lcr.stimulus: Can you believe the cafeteria is closed again?
lcr.tone_note: complaint / 表面問句實為抱怨
options: Yes, it usually closes around nine in the evening.
I know, it's so frustrating. Let's eat somewhere else.
Great, I really love the food they serve there.
No, I don't believe anything you ever say.
lcr.why: 這句是抱怨而非真的詢問,應同理並提出替代方案,不能照字面回答。

lcr.stimulus: Would you mind passing me that stapler on your desk?
options: Not at all, here it is for you.
Yes, actually I would mind that a lot.
The stapler seems to be made of metal.
I'm not really sure where your desk is.
lcr.why: 委婉請求應答應並遞出,回答否定詞反而顯得無禮。

lcr.stimulus: Wait, you're telling me the exam got moved to Monday?
options: Yes, the professor announced it this morning.
Mondays are usually pretty busy for me.
I'm not really telling you anything at all.
The exam has fifty multiple-choice questions.
lcr.why: 說話者在驚訝地求證,應直接確認消息屬實。

lcr.stimulus: Why don't we take the express bus instead of the local one?
options: The bus is usually painted blue and white.
Because I have never ridden a bus before.
Good idea, it'll save us about twenty minutes.
I don't know why you always ask me that.
lcr.why: Why don't we 是提議,應答應並補充好處,而非照字面解釋原因。

lcr.stimulus: This soup is completely cold, and we've been waiting forever.
options: Cold soup is actually a specialty in some places.
Yes, forever really is a very long time.
The restaurant does have a lovely view outside.
I'll tell the waiter now and ask for a fresh bowl.
lcr.why: 對方在抱怨餐點,應回應並著手處理問題。

lcr.stimulus: Excuse me, could you check if you have this jacket in a medium?
options: This jacket comes in three different colors.
Sure, let me go look in the back for you.
A medium is a fairly common size, I think.
I actually bought a jacket like that last winter.
lcr.why: 這是請店員查庫存的請求,應答應去查。

lcr.stimulus: Hold on, did you seriously walk all the way here in the rain?
options: Yeah, my umbrella broke, so I had no choice.
Rain forms when water vapor cools and condenses.
No, I have honestly never been serious once.
Walking is really good exercise for your legs.
lcr.why: 對方驚訝地確認,應回答並解釋原因。

lcr.stimulus: You've been coughing all week. Maybe you should see a doctor?
options: Doctors usually train for many years first.
A cough is a reflex controlled by the brain.
You're right, I'll make an appointment tomorrow.
I have actually been to that clinic once before.
lcr.why: 對方建議就醫,應接受建議並表示會行動。

lcr.stimulus: The library Wi-Fi keeps dropping every five minutes today.
options: Wi-Fi actually stands for wireless fidelity.
The library holds over a million books inside.
Five minutes is just enough time to boil an egg.
Same here, I can barely load anything. Let's report it.
lcr.why: 對方抱怨網路問題,應表達同感並提出解決辦法。

lcr.stimulus: Do you think you could drop me off at the station on your way?
options: The station was built about fifty years ago.
Of course, hop in. It's right on my way.
Trains are generally faster than the buses.
I think train stations are usually quite crowded.
lcr.why: 這是請求搭便車,應答應並說順路。

lcr.stimulus: Wait, they're charging us twenty dollars just for parking?
options: Yes, unfortunately the rate went up last month.
Parking lots are usually made of thick concrete.
Twenty is an even number, if you think about it.
I really do enjoy going for drives on weekends.
lcr.why: 對方驚訝地確認高價,應證實並補充說明。

lcr.stimulus: How about we grab coffee after class and go over the notes?
options: Coffee contains a chemical known as caffeine.
Notes really should always be written neatly.
Sounds perfect, I could use a break anyway.
Our class ends at exactly three o'clock today.
lcr.why: 對方提議一起讀書,應接受邀約。

lcr.stimulus: I ordered this online two weeks ago and it still hasn't arrived.
options: Online shopping has grown very popular lately.
Two weeks is exactly fourteen days, you know.
I really love getting packages in the mail.
That's unacceptable. You should ask for a refund.
lcr.why: 對方抱怨延遲到貨,應同理並建議退款。

lcr.stimulus: Could we get the check whenever you have a moment?
options: Checks were once more common than credit cards.
The dessert menu has some lovely options too.
Of course, I'll bring it right over for you.
A moment is really just a very short time.
lcr.why: 這是請服務生結帳的請求,應答應馬上處理。

lcr.stimulus: You mean the whole lecture was cancelled and nobody told us?
options: Lectures here usually last about ninety minutes.
Nobody is a pronoun, grammatically speaking.
I really do admire that professor's research.
Afraid so. The email must have gone to spam.
lcr.why: 對方驚訝地確認課程取消,應證實並解釋原因。

lcr.stimulus: If you're feeling that tired, why not just call it a night?
options: Nighttime is when most people tend to sleep.
Yeah, you're right. I'll head to bed early.
I called an old friend on the phone last night.
Being tired can really affect your concentration.
lcr.why: 對方建議早點休息,應接受建議。

lcr.stimulus: Wow, another twenty pages of reading for tomorrow?
lcr.tone_note: complaint — 抱怨作業繁重,非確認頁數
options: I know, right? There's no way I'm finishing all that tonight.
Yes, exactly twenty pages, chapters four and five.
Tomorrow is Thursday, I think.
You should read more for fun, honestly.
lcr.why: 語調顯示這是對繁重作業的抱怨,應以共鳴回應,而非確認頁數或糾正星期。

lcr.stimulus: I don't suppose there's any chance of an extension on the essay?
lcr.tone_note: request — 委婉請求延期(I don't suppose...)
options: No, I don't suppose so either.
The essay should be about five pages.
Well, if you can hand it in by Monday, that should be fine.
Yes, extensions are a chance to grow.
lcr.why: 「I don't suppose...」是委婉請求延期,應直接回應是否給延期,而非附和語氣或談字數。

lcr.stimulus: Ugh, my roommate's alarm has been going off for like ten minutes.
lcr.tone_note: complaint — 抱怨室友鬧鐘,想要同情或辦法
options: Alarms usually ring for a minute or so.
That's so annoying. Have you tried knocking on his door?
Ten minutes is a good nap length.
I set three alarms every morning.
lcr.why: 這是對室友鬧鐘的抱怨,回應應表達同情並提供辦法,干擾項只抓「十分鐘」等字面。

lcr.stimulus: Excuse me, would you mind keeping it down a little?
options: No, I wouldn't mind at all.
Down where? The lower floor?
I keep my keys in my bag.
Oh, sorry — we didn't realize we were being loud.
lcr.why: 「Would you mind keeping it down」是請求安靜,恰當回應是道歉配合,字面回「不介意」反而失禮。

lcr.stimulus: Hold on — they closed the dining hall for the whole weekend?
lcr.tone_note: surprise — 驚訝並想確認餐廳關閉
options: Yeah, I couldn't believe it either. Renovations, apparently.
The dining hall serves breakfast at seven.
You should hold the door for people.
Weekends are two days long.
lcr.why: 語調表達驚訝並想確認,應回應共感並補充原因,而非談供餐時間或字面天數。

lcr.stimulus: We're all grabbing coffee after the meeting — you in?
lcr.tone_note: invitation — 邀約一起去喝咖啡
options: The meeting is in room 200.
Coffee has caffeine in it.
Sure, I'd love to. Let me just pack up my stuff.
I'm in my second year.
lcr.why: 「You in?」是邀約,應接受或婉拒,干擾項誤解為問樓層、年級或談咖啡成分。

lcr.stimulus: So the midterm's gonna cover everything up to chapter six, right?
options: Midterms are stressful, aren't they?
That's right — six and everything before it.
Chapter six is about photosynthesis.
Right is the opposite of left.
lcr.why: 這是尋求確認考試範圍,應直接證實,而非談壓力或章節內容。

lcr.stimulus: You look like you're really struggling with those boxes.
lcr.tone_note: offer — 委婉提議幫忙搬箱子
options: Yes, I do look tired today.
The boxes are full of textbooks.
Struggling builds character, they say.
Would you? That'd be a huge help, thanks.
lcr.why: 這句其實是委婉提議幫忙,恰當回應是接受,而非只承認自己累或談箱子內容。

lcr.stimulus: Have you considered switching your major to statistics?
options: Statistics has a lot of numbers.
No, I haven't considered anything.
I hadn't, but I'll definitely think it over.
My major is on the third floor.
lcr.why: 「Have you considered...」是建議,應表態會考慮,干擾項答成談數字或誤解 major。

lcr.stimulus: Any idea if this book can be renewed online?
lcr.tone_note: request — 詢問能否線上續借
options: It should be — just log into your library account.
Books are made of paper.
Yes, I have lots of ideas.
The book looks pretty new.
lcr.why: 這是詢問能否線上續借,應給方法,干擾項抓「idea」「new」等字面。

lcr.stimulus: This is the third time the campus Wi-Fi's dropped today.
lcr.tone_note: complaint — 抱怨 Wi-Fi 不穩
options: Wi-Fi stands for wireless fidelity.
Three is a prime number.
You should drop that class.
Ugh, mine too. I've been meaning to report it.
lcr.why: 這是抱怨網路不穩,應共鳴或提報修,干擾項只抓「third」「drop」等字面。

lcr.stimulus: Wait, the presentation's due this Friday, not next?
lcr.tone_note: surprise — 驚訝確認報告期限
options: Presentations should have slides.
Yep, this Friday — I was shocked too.
Friday comes after Thursday.
Next time, present more slowly.
lcr.why: 語調表驚訝並確認期限,應證實並共感,而非談投影片或字面星期。

lcr.stimulus: You should totally run for club president this year.
options: The president lives in the White House.
Running is good exercise.
Me? I'm flattered, but I've got way too much on my plate.
Yes, this year has twelve months.
lcr.why: 這是鼓勵參選的建議,合宜回應是接受或婉拒,干擾項抓「president」「run」字面。

lcr.stimulus: It's getting kind of late — do you think you could turn the music down?
lcr.tone_note: request — 委婉請求關小音樂
options: Oh, of course — I didn't realize the time.
No, I don't think I could.
The music is jazz.
Turn left at the hallway.
lcr.why: 這是委婉請求關小音樂,應答應,字面回「我沒辦法」或談曲風都不對。

lcr.stimulus: Just to be clear, you want the citations in APA format?
options: Clarity is important in writing.
Yes, the format looks nice.
APA is near the library.
Correct — APA, seventh edition, please.
lcr.why: 這是確認引用格式,應明確回覆,干擾項只抓「clear」或含糊帶過。

lcr.stimulus: No way — you actually ran the whole marathon?
lcr.tone_note: surprise — 驚訝確認跑完馬拉松
options: Marathons are 26.2 miles.
I did! Still can't believe it myself.
There's a way around the building.
Yes, running is allowed here.
lcr.why: 這是驚訝並想確認,應證實並附和情緒,干擾項答里程或抓「way/run」。

lcr.stimulus: I have no clue how to start this research paper.
lcr.tone_note: complaint/request — 表達卡關,暗含求助
options: Why don't you start with a rough outline? That always helps me.
Papers are due at the end of term.
A clue is a hint.
Start the car with the key.
lcr.why: 這是表達卡關並暗含求助,應給建議,干擾項抓「clue」「start」字面。

lcr.stimulus: This is the quiet study zone, isn't it?
lcr.tone_note: confirmation — 確認是否為安靜區
options: Quiet is the opposite of loud.
The zone has good lighting.
Yes, I studied all night.
It is, yeah — no talking past this point.
lcr.why: 「isn't it」是確認此處是否為安靜區,應證實並補規則,干擾項抓字面。

lcr.stimulus: Seriously? Another parking ticket on my windshield?
options: Tickets can be bought at the box office.
Ah man, that's rough. Where'd you park this time?
Windshields protect you from wind.
Yes, parking is available on campus.
lcr.why: 這是抱怨又被開罰單,應同情關心,干擾項抓「ticket」「windshield」字面。

lcr.stimulus: You're pretty good with design software, aren't you?
lcr.tone_note: request — 恭維鋪陳,實為求助
options: Yes, I am pretty good.
Software needs updates sometimes.
A little — did you need help with a poster or something?
Design is on the second floor.
lcr.why: 這句是鋪陳求助的開場,敏銳回應應主動問要幫什麼,而非只承認自己厲害。

lcr.stimulus: I'm heading to the store — need me to pick up anything?
options: Actually, yeah — could you grab some milk?
Stores close at nine.
No, I don't need directions there.
Pick up your clothes off the floor.
lcr.why: 這是主動提議代購,應接受並說要什麼,干擾項誤解或抓「pick up」字面。

lcr.stimulus: You might want to double-check your data before submitting.
lcr.tone_note: suggestion — 委婉建議檢查資料
options: Data is plural for datum.
No, I don't want to double anything.
The check is in the mail.
Good point — I'll go over it one more time.
lcr.why: 「You might want to...」是委婉建議檢查,應接受,干擾項抓「data」「check」字面。

lcr.stimulus: Is there any way I could see a doctor today?
options: Doctors go to medical school.
Let me check — we might have an opening at four.
Yes, there's a way out back.
I can see you clearly.
lcr.why: 這是請求當天看診,應查看能否安排,干擾項抓「way」「see」字面。

lcr.stimulus: Hang on, you got a perfect score on that exam?
options: Exams are held in the gym.
Hang your coat on the hook.
I know, I was stunned when I saw it.
A score is twenty of something.
lcr.why: 這是驚訝確認滿分,應附和情緒證實,干擾項抓「hang」「score」字面。

lcr.stimulus: Every single computer in here is taken.
lcr.tone_note: complaint — 抱怨電腦全被占用
options: Computers run on electricity.
Tell me about it. Maybe try the lab upstairs?
Yes, take one home with you.
Singles cost less than doubles.
lcr.why: 這是抱怨電腦全被占用,應共鳴並給替代方案,干擾項抓「taken」「single」字面。

lcr.stimulus: We're still meeting at six, right?
options: Six is half of twelve.
Meetings can be productive.
Right, take a right at the door.
As far as I know, yeah — same room too.
lcr.why: 這是確認開會時間,應證實,干擾項抓「six」「right」字面。

lcr.stimulus: Did you seriously eat my leftovers again?
lcr.tone_note: complaint — 抱怨剩菜被吃掉(反詰)
options: Oh no — that was yours? I'm really sorry.
Yes, leftovers are food from before.
Seriously means for real.
I ate at the dining hall.
lcr.why: 語調是抱怨被吃掉剩菜,應道歉,而非答字面或解釋詞義。

lcr.stimulus: Would it be alright if I recorded today's lecture?
options: Records used to be made of vinyl.
No, I'm not alright today.
That's fine, as long as it's just for your own studying.
Lectures are one hour long.
lcr.why: 這是請求錄音許可,應給予條件式同意,干擾項抓「record」或誤解「alright」。

lcr.stimulus: A hundred and twenty dollars for one textbook?!
options: Textbooks contain chapters.
I know, it's ridiculous. Have you checked for a used copy?
A hundred and twenty is a big number.
Yes, the store sells books.
lcr.why: 語調是對高價的震驚抱怨,應共鳴並建議找二手,干擾項只抓數字或講廢話。

lcr.stimulus: Maybe we should split the project into two parts?
lcr.tone_note: suggestion — 提議拆分報告分工
options: Splits are hard in gymnastics.
Projects have deadlines.
Two comes after one.
Yeah, that makes sense — you take the research, I'll do the slides?
lcr.why: 這是提議分工,應回應如何分,干擾項抓「split」或講常識。

lcr.stimulus: You're the new resident in 4B, aren't you?
options: That's me — just moved in yesterday.
New things are exciting.
No, residents pay rent.
4B is a pencil grade.
lcr.why: 「aren't you」是確認身分,應證實,干擾項抓「new」「4B」字面。

lcr.stimulus: I can never seem to find the journals section.
lcr.tone_note: request — 委婉求助找期刊區
options: Journals are for writing feelings.
Yes, seeking is hard.
Oh, it's on the second floor — I'll walk you there.
I found my keys earlier.
lcr.why: 這是委婉求助找期刊區,應指路帶領,干擾項抓「journal」「find」字面。

lcr.stimulus: There's an empty seat here if you're looking for one.
options: Seats have four legs usually.
No, I'm not looking at anything.
Empty means nothing inside.
Oh, thanks — mind if I join you?
lcr.why: 這是主動讓座,應道謝入座,干擾項抓「looking」「empty」字面。

lcr.stimulus: I studied so hard, but I still barely passed.
lcr.tone_note: complaint — 表達努力卻結果不佳的沮喪
options: Passing means you succeeded.
That must be frustrating. Want to go over what went wrong together?
Hard work builds muscle.
Yes, you studied a lot.
lcr.why: 這是表達努力卻結果不佳的沮喪,應同理並提供協助,干擾項答字面或無幫助。

lcr.stimulus: You think the fundraiser went okay?
lcr.tone_note: confirmation — 尋求對活動的安慰肯定
options: Funds are money, basically.
Okay is spelled O-K.
Are you kidding? Everyone loved it — you did great.
Events need planning.
lcr.why: 這是尋求安慰肯定,應給予正向回應,干擾項抓「fund」或講廢話。

lcr.stimulus: It's pouring out there and you don't have an umbrella?
options: Nope — mind if we share yours to the bus stop?
Rain falls from clouds.
Yes, umbrellas are useful.
Pour the water slowly.
lcr.why: 這句其實暗示要分享雨傘,合宜回應是接受合撐,而非講常識或抓「pour」。

lcr.stimulus: Sorry, could you go over that last part one more time?
options: The last part is the ending.
No, I only say things once.
Go over the bridge, then turn.
Of course — so, the key idea is that supply affects price.
lcr.why: 這是請求再講一次,應重述重點,干擾項抓「go over」或無禮拒絕。

lcr.stimulus: My laptop won't connect to the printer no matter what I try.
lcr.tone_note: complaint/request — 抱怨兼求助印表機
options: Printers use ink or toner.
Let's take a look — have you installed the campus printer driver?
Laptops sit on your lap.
Yes, trying is important.
lcr.why: 這是抱怨兼求助印表機問題,應著手協助排查,干擾項講常識或無幫助。

lcr.stimulus: This desk lamp keeps flickering.
lcr.tone_note: complaint — 抱怨檯燈閃爍,暗示想解決
options: Lamps give off light.
Flickering is a movie term.
Annoying — why don't you grab that desk by the window instead?
Yes, the desk is wooden.
lcr.why: 這是抱怨檯燈閃爍暗示想解決,應提替代座位,干擾項講常識或抓「flicker」。

lcr.stimulus: Wait, you're saying the deadline got pushed back a week?
lcr.tone_note: surprise — 驚訝確認期限延後
options: That's right — you've got until next Friday now.
Pushing is a physical action.
Deadlines are important.
A week has seven days.
lcr.why: 這是驚訝確認期限延後,應證實新期限,干擾項抓「push」或講常識。

lcr.stimulus: We could really use another pair of hands at Saturday's event.
options: I'm free Saturday — count me in.
Hands have five fingers.
Yes, pairs come in twos.
Events can be fun.
lcr.why: 「could use another pair of hands」是委婉招募幫手,應主動答應,干擾項抓「hands/pair」字面。

lcr.stimulus: Do you always leave the kitchen this messy?
lcr.tone_note: complaint — 抱怨廚房髒亂(反詰)
options: Yes, always means every time.
Kitchens are for cooking.
You're right, sorry — I'll clean up right now.
No, the kitchen is down the hall.
lcr.why: 語調是抱怨廚房髒亂,應道歉並收拾,而非答字面或誤解成問位置。

lcr.stimulus: Wow, these headphones are on sale? I've been eyeing 'em for months.
lcr.tone_note: 驚喜地發現心儀商品竟然特價
options: Right? Better grab a pair before they're gone.
Sorry, we don't sell headphones.
You should listen to more music.
Eyes are the window to the soul.
lcr.why: 顧客興奮發現特價,同伴附和並建議快買才自然。

lcr.stimulus: Um, this receipt says I got charged twice for the coffee.
lcr.tone_note: 委婉指出帳單被重複收費
options: Coffee is two dollars each.
Oh, my mistake—let me refund one of those for you.
You must really like coffee.
Receipts are recyclable, by the way.
lcr.why: 顧客指出被重複收費,店員應道歉並退款才對。

lcr.stimulus: Do you guys take returns without a receipt?
lcr.tone_note: 詢問無收據時的退貨政策
options: Returns are on the second floor.
We don't sell receipts here.
We can do store credit, but not a cash refund.
You should always keep your receipts.
lcr.why: 顧客問無收據能否退貨,店員說明可換購物金才切題。

lcr.stimulus: Is it just me, or is this line barely moving?
options: The line forms on the right.
It's just you, I think.
A line is made up of people.
Tell me about it—I've been here twenty minutes.
lcr.why: 顧客抱怨隊伍不動,對方表達同感附和才自然。

lcr.stimulus: Excuse me, would you happen to have this in a size up?
lcr.tone_note: 委婉請店員找大一號尺寸
options: Sizes are so confusing these days.
Let me check the back for you real quick.
That size looks fine on you.
We happen to sell all kinds of things.
lcr.why: 顧客委婉問有無大一號,店員應去查貨才恰當。

lcr.stimulus: These are cute and all, but forty bucks for socks?
lcr.tone_note: 覺得襪子太貴、猶豫要不要買
options: Yeah, that's steep—wanna check the cheaper rack?
Socks always come in pairs.
Forty is a nice round number.
They'd look great with those shoes.
lcr.why: 顧客嫌襪子太貴,同伴附和並提議看便宜的才切題。

lcr.stimulus: Sorry, I ordered the salad, not the fries.
options: Fries are our specialty, though.
Salad is healthier anyway.
Well, enjoy your meal!
My apologies—I'll bring your salad right out.
lcr.why: 客人指出上錯菜,服務生應致歉並補上沙拉。

lcr.stimulus: Is there any chance we could sit by the window?
lcr.tone_note: 委婉請求換到靠窗座位
options: Windows let in a lot of light.
The window seats really are the best.
Sure, let me see if one's open.
There's no chance of rain today.
lcr.why: 客人委婉要求靠窗座位,服務生應答應去查看。

lcr.stimulus: Whoa, this is way spicier than I expected.
options: Spicy food is good for you.
We're famous for our spice.
Want me to grab you some more water?
You expected it to be mild?
lcr.why: 客人驚訝太辣,同伴體貼地提議倒水才自然。

lcr.stimulus: Could you tell the kitchen to hold the onions?
options: Onions can make you cry.
The kitchen's pretty busy right now.
We hold the onions in the pantry.
No problem, I'll let them know.
lcr.why: 客人請求不要放洋蔥,服務生應答應轉告廚房。

lcr.stimulus: We've been waiting quite a while for our food.
options: I'm sorry—let me go check on that for you.
Well, good food takes time.
Did you order a while ago?
Waiting builds patience, they say.
lcr.why: 客人抱怨等太久,服務生應致歉並去催餐。

lcr.stimulus: Do you know if this dish has any nuts in it?
lcr.tone_note: 因過敏疑慮而詢問成分
options: Nuts are a great source of protein.
Let me double-check with the chef to be safe.
This one's our bestseller.
I don't really know much about nuts.
lcr.why: 客人可能過敏而問成分,服務生應向廚師確認才負責。

lcr.stimulus: Any idea why we've been stopped for so long?
lcr.tone_note: 焦急詢問列車延誤原因
options: Trains stop at stations, you know.
Stopping is totally normal.
I actually like sitting still.
There's signal trouble ahead—should be moving soon.
lcr.why: 乘客關心為何久停,對方說明原因並安撫才切題。

lcr.stimulus: Excuse me, is anyone sitting here?
lcr.tone_note: 委婉詢問座位是否空著
options: Someone was sitting there earlier.
Seats are for sitting, after all.
Nope, it's all yours.
I actually prefer to stand.
lcr.why: 對方問座位空不空,回答沒人請坐最自然。

lcr.stimulus: Could you maybe slow down a little?
lcr.tone_note: 乘客緊張,委婉請司機開慢點
options: Slow and steady wins the race.
Sorry, didn't realize I was going so fast.
The speed limit here is sixty.
I can't exactly slow down time.
lcr.why: 乘客委婉請開慢,司機應道歉並放慢才恰當。

lcr.stimulus: Wait, this is the last train tonight?
lcr.tone_note: 驚訝並擔心錯過末班車
options: Yeah, so we'd better hurry to catch it.
Trains do run on tracks.
It's a lovely night out.
The first train's at five, though.
lcr.why: 對方驚覺是末班車,回應提醒快點趕上才切題。

lcr.stimulus: Do you happen to know which stop's closest to the museum?
options: Get off at Central—it's a two-minute walk from there.
Museums are super educational.
I happen to love museums myself.
Stops are announced onboard.
lcr.why: 乘客問哪站離博物館近,對方給出明確站名才有幫助。

lcr.stimulus: Ugh, we just missed it, didn't we?
options: Missing things is part of life.
We didn't miss anything important.
Afraid so—next one's in fifteen minutes.
You should've run faster.
lcr.why: 對方懊惱錯過車,回應確認並告知下班時間才體貼。

lcr.stimulus: Doctor, is it normal for it to still hurt after two weeks?
options: Two weeks is fourteen days.
Let's take another look to be sure it's healing right.
Pain is just weakness leaving the body.
Well, normal is a relative term.
lcr.why: 病人擔心還在痛,醫生應再檢查確認才負責。

lcr.stimulus: I've been feeling kinda dizzy since this morning.
lcr.tone_note: 陳述不適、暗示需要協助
options: Mornings can be rough, huh.
Dizzy is a funny-sounding word.
You look perfectly fine to me.
Why don't you sit down and I'll get you some water?
lcr.why: 對方說頭暈,體貼地請他坐下並倒水才恰當。

lcr.stimulus: Should I be worried about this rash, do you think?
options: Worrying is bad for your health.
Rashes are red sometimes.
It's probably nothing, but a doctor could check to be safe.
I've never had a rash myself.
lcr.why: 對方問是否該擔心,建議看醫生確認最妥當。

lcr.stimulus: Is the pharmacy still open at this hour?
lcr.tone_note: 急需拿藥、詢問營業時間
options: It closes at ten, so you've got about twenty minutes.
Pharmacies sell all kinds of medicine.
This hour is pretty late, huh.
I take my vitamins every day.
lcr.why: 對方急問藥局是否還開,告知關門時間與剩餘時間才有用。

lcr.stimulus: Ow, I think I twisted my ankle back there.
lcr.tone_note: 陳述受傷、暗示需要攙扶
options: Ankles are pretty delicate joints.
You should watch where you walk.
Twisting's actually good exercise.
Here, lean on me and let's get you sitting down.
lcr.why: 對方扭到腳,主動攙扶並讓他坐下才是恰當回應。

lcr.stimulus: Have you been getting enough sleep? You look wiped.
options: Sleep is measured in hours.
Honestly, no—I've been pulling late nights all week.
Aw, you look great too.
Wiping's for tables, silly.
lcr.why: 對方關心你沒睡好,坦白說熬夜才是自然回應。

lcr.stimulus: Wait, our flight got pushed to tomorrow?
lcr.tone_note: 驚訝又沮喪航班被延誤
options: Planes have wings, you know.
Yeah, weather's a mess—guess we're stuck for the night.
Well, tomorrow is another day.
You're the one who booked it.
lcr.why: 對方震驚航班延到明天,回應說明原因並接受現況才切題。

lcr.stimulus: Excuse me, could you take a quick photo of us?
lcr.tone_note: 委婉請陌生人幫忙拍照
options: Photos really capture memories.
I'm not much of a photographer.
Cameras can be expensive.
Sure! Okay, say cheese.
lcr.why: 遊客請人幫忙拍照,爽快答應才自然。

lcr.stimulus: Is it just me, or does this room smell kinda musty?
lcr.tone_note: 抱怨房間有霉味、暗示想換房
options: Yeah, let's ask the front desk for a different room.
Smells travel through the air.
I think musty is a cozy smell.
You've got a really good nose.
lcr.why: 對方抱怨房間有霉味,提議去櫃檯換房才對應。

lcr.stimulus: Do you know how far the beach is from here?
options: Beaches are covered in sand.
Oh, I just love the beach.
About a ten-minute walk straight down this road.
Far is kind of a matter of opinion.
lcr.why: 對方問海灘多遠,給出步行時間與方向才有幫助。

lcr.stimulus: We're not gonna make the tour if we don't leave now.
options: Tours are really informative.
We've got all the time in the world.
Leaving is always hard to do.
You're right—let me grab my bag and we'll go.
lcr.why: 對方催促再不走會趕不上,回應立刻準備出發才切題。

lcr.stimulus: Ugh, I think I left my passport at the last hotel.
options: Passports are blue in some countries.
Okay, don't panic—let's call them right now.
You've really gotta be more careful.
Most hotels have a lost-and-found.
lcr.why: 對方驚慌把護照忘在旅館,安撫並立刻打電話才是好回應。

lcr.stimulus: You're not seriously wearing that to the party, are you?
lcr.tone_note: 委婉表達不贊同、暗示該換衣服
options: Parties are always fun.
Yes, I am seriously wearing it.
Too much? I can go change into something else.
Aw, you look nice too.
lcr.why: 對方委婉嫌你的穿著,回應意識到並願意換裝才對。

lcr.stimulus: We should totally grab coffee sometime!
options: I'd love that—how's next week?
Coffee's got a lot of caffeine.
Sometime isn't a real time, though.
I can't really grab things.
lcr.why: 對方提議改天喝咖啡,熱情回應並約時間才自然。

lcr.stimulus: Did you really not get my message?
options: I swear, my phone's been acting up all day.
Messages are sent digitally.
I get tons of messages, though.
Your message was pretty long.
lcr.why: 對方半信半疑你沒收到訊息,解釋手機出問題才化解。

lcr.stimulus: Hey, thanks for covering for me yesterday.
options: Blankets keep you nice and warm.
Yesterday was a Tuesday.
Don't mention it—that's what friends are for.
You totally owe me one.
lcr.why: 對方道謝你幫忙代班,謙虛回應才得體。

lcr.stimulus: Is everything okay? You've been kinda quiet tonight.
options: Quiet is the opposite of loud.
There's a nice moon out tonight.
I'm always this talkative.
Yeah, just got a lot on my mind lately.
lcr.why: 對方關心你今晚很安靜,坦白說心事重重才是真誠回應。

lcr.stimulus: You really didn't have to bring anything, you know.
lcr.tone_note: 客套地感謝對方帶禮物
options: Bringing gifts is the polite thing.
I know, but I wanted to—hope you like it.
Fine, then I'll just take it back.
I didn't bring all that much.
lcr.why: 主人客氣說不用帶東西,回應其實是想帶並送上才得體。

lcr.stimulus: Would you mind covering my shift on Saturday?
options: Shifts can get pretty long.
Sure, I'm free Saturday—no problem.
Saturday comes once a week.
Oh, I mind plenty of things.
lcr.why: 同事請你代週六的班,爽快答應才自然。

lcr.stimulus: Um, my paycheck seems a little short this month.
options: Paychecks are printed on paper.
Money's tight for everyone lately.
You should really save more.
Let me pull up your hours and sort that out.
lcr.why: 員工委婉反映薪水短少,主管應查工時並處理才負責。

lcr.stimulus: Is it cool if I take off a bit early today?
options: Yeah, things are slow—go ahead.
Cool means a low temperature.
The early bird catches the worm.
But you just got here.
lcr.why: 員工委婉問能否早退,主管見不忙而答應才恰當。

lcr.stimulus: You've been late three times this week.
lcr.tone_note: 主管委婉責備屢次遲到
options: Three's a lucky number, right?
A week has seven days.
I know, I'm sorry—the bus keeps getting delayed.
But I've been early loads of times.
lcr.why: 主管指出你屢次遲到,認錯並說明原因才得體。

lcr.stimulus: Could you show me how the register works?
options: Registers hold all the money.
Eh, you'll figure it out.
Of course—come on over and I'll walk you through it.
Work can be tough sometimes.
lcr.why: 新人請教收銀機操作,主動示範教學才是好回應。

lcr.stimulus: Do you think I could pick up some extra hours?
options: For sure—we could use the help this weekend.
An hour has sixty minutes.
You already work quite a lot.
Picking things up is good exercise.
lcr.why: 員工想多排班,主管回應正好需要人手才切題。

conversation.setting: A student talks with an academic advisor about changing her class schedule.

turns.text: Hi, I was hoping to switch out of my Tuesday morning chemistry lab. Is that still possible?
Let me check. The add-drop deadline is this Friday, so you still have time. What section were you thinking of moving to?
Ideally the Thursday afternoon one. The morning slot conflicts with my part-time job.
Thursday afternoon does have two open seats right now, but it fills up fast. I'd really recommend registering today if you can.
Great. Do I need my professor's signature for that?
Not for a lab section swap. You can do it yourself through the online portal, but make sure you submit before midnight Friday.
Perfect. I'll do it as soon as I get back to my dorm. Thanks so much.

questions.q: Why does the student come to see the advisor?
options: To drop out of her chemistry course entirely.
To switch her lab to a different time slot.
To ask for an extension on the deadline.
To get a signature from her professor.
questions.why: 學生想把週二早上的實驗課換到別的時段。

questions.q: What does the advisor tell her to do?
options: Register for the new section before Friday midnight.
Wait until after the add-drop deadline.
Get her professor's signature first.
Call her job to change her work hours.
questions.why: 顧問要她在週五午夜前透過線上系統完成登記。

conversation.setting: A waiter takes an order from a customer at a small restaurant.

turns.text: Good evening. Have you had a chance to look at the menu, or do you need a few more minutes?
I think I'm ready. I'll have the grilled salmon, but could I get the vegetables instead of the fries?
Absolutely. The salmon comes with a lemon butter sauce. Is that all right, or would you prefer it on the side?
On the side would be great, thanks. I'm trying to watch how much butter I eat.
No problem at all. And can I get you anything to drink?
Just water for now. Oh, and how long does the salmon usually take? I'm meeting a friend at eight.
It's only about fifteen minutes, so you'll be finished with plenty of time to spare.

questions.q: What change does the customer make to her order?
options: She asks for fries instead of vegetables.
She asks for vegetables instead of fries.
She orders the chicken instead of the salmon.
She adds extra butter sauce to the dish.
questions.why: 顧客把附餐的薯條換成蔬菜。

questions.q: Why does the customer ask about the cooking time?
options: She has plans to meet someone at eight.
She thinks the food is taking too long.
She wants to cancel her order.
She is worried the kitchen is closing soon.
questions.why: 她八點要和朋友碰面,想確認是否來得及。

conversation.setting: A customer tries to return a pair of headphones at an electronics store.

turns.text: Hi, I'd like to return these headphones. They stopped working after only three days.
I'm sorry to hear that. Do you happen to have the receipt with you?
I do, right here. I bought them last Saturday.
Thank you. Since it's within our thirty-day window, I can offer you a full refund or an exchange. Which would you prefer?
Honestly, I really liked the sound quality, so I'd rather just exchange them for the same model.
Sure. Let me grab a new pair from the stockroom. If you'd like, I can also test them at the counter before you leave.
That would be great, actually. I'd hate to walk out with another broken pair.

questions.q: What is the man's problem?
options: He lost the receipt for his headphones.
He was charged the wrong price at checkout.
He bought the wrong model by mistake.
His headphones stopped working after a few days.
questions.why: 他的耳機用了三天就故障了。

questions.q: What will the man most likely do next?
options: Ask for a full refund and leave.
Go find the receipt in his car.
Test a new pair at the counter.
Choose a completely different model.
questions.why: 他同意在櫃檯測試店員拿來的新耳機。

conversation.setting: A patient talks with a nurse at a campus health center.

turns.text: Hi there, what brings you in to the health center today?
I've had a sore throat and a bit of a fever since the weekend. It doesn't seem to be getting better.
Let's take your temperature first. Have you been drinking plenty of fluids and getting rest?
I've been trying to, but I have three midterms this week, so I haven't slept much.
That's understandable, but rest really is the best medicine right now. Your temperature is slightly high, so I'd like the doctor to take a quick look.
Is it going to take long? My next exam is at two.
It shouldn't. The doctor has an opening in about ten minutes, so you'll make it with time to spare.

questions.q: What symptoms does the man describe?
options: A headache and an upset stomach.
A cough and a runny nose.
Dizziness and blurred vision.
A sore throat and a fever.
questions.why: 他說自己喉嚨痛又發燒。

questions.q: Why hasn't the man been getting enough rest?
options: He has been working a night shift.
He has been traveling all week.
He has three midterm exams this week.
He lives with very noisy roommates.
questions.why: 他這週有三場期中考,所以睡得很少。

conversation.setting: Two friends discuss plans for an upcoming weekend trip to the coast.

turns.text: So, are we still driving up to the coast this Saturday, or taking the train?
I was thinking the train, actually. Parking near the beach is a nightmare, and it's twenty-five dollars a day.
Good point. The train is only twelve dollars each way, and we could both just relax instead of driving.
Exactly. The earliest one leaves at seven-thirty, though. Do you think that's too early?
Not at all. If we catch that one, we'll have the whole day there. Should I book the tickets tonight?
Please do. And let's pack sandwiches so we don't spend a fortune on food once we arrive.
Deal. I'll bring the drinks too. This is going to be a great trip.

questions.q: Why do they decide to take the train instead of driving?
options: The train is much faster than the car.
Neither of them owns a car.
The road to the coast is closed.
Parking is expensive and hard to find.
questions.why: 海邊停車又貴又難找,所以他們改搭火車。

questions.q: What does the woman agree to do?
options: Drive everyone to the station.
Pack the sandwiches for lunch.
Book the train tickets tonight.
Wake everyone up early tomorrow.
questions.why: 她答應今晚就把火車票訂好。

conversation.setting: A student invites a classmate to join a study group and a party.

turns.text: Hey, a few of us are forming a study group for the statistics final. Would you want to join?
I'd love to. That class has been really tough on me, honestly. When are you all meeting?
Wednesday evenings at the library, starting at six. We usually go for about two hours.
That works perfectly with my schedule. Should I bring anything, like practice problems?
If you have the old quizzes, that would be super helpful. Oh, and we're throwing a small get-together Friday night to celebrate finishing exams. You're welcome to come.
That sounds fun. Text me the address and I'll definitely try to stop by after my last exam.
Will do. It should be a great way to unwind after such a stressful week.

questions.q: What is the man mainly inviting the woman to do?
options: Join a study group for a final exam.
Help him tutor a statistics class.
Sign up for a new course together.
Meet with a professor after class.
questions.why: 男生主要邀她加入統計期末考的讀書會。

questions.q: What does the man ask the woman to bring?
options: Snacks and drinks for the group.
Old quizzes and practice problems.
Her laptop and a calculator.
The address of the Friday party.
questions.why: 他請她帶舊考卷和練習題。

conversation.setting: A student talks with a registration office clerk about a course that is full.

turns.text: Hi, I'm trying to register for Introduction to Psychology, but the system keeps saying the section is full. Is there anything you can do?
Let me check for you. Yes, that section reached capacity yesterday. However, there's a second section that meets on Tuesday and Thursday mornings.
Oh, mornings are tough for me because I have a part-time job. Is there a waitlist for the first section instead?
There is. I can add you to it, but I should warn you that eight people are already ahead of you, so a spot may not open up.
Eight? That doesn't sound promising. Maybe I'll take the morning section after all and try to switch my work shift.
That might be the safer choice. Would you like me to enroll you in the morning section now while seats are still available?
Yes, please go ahead. I'd rather lock in a spot than risk missing the class entirely.

questions.q: Why does the man go to the office?
options: To drop a psychology course
To register for a course that appears full
To complain about a clerk
To pay his tuition
questions.why: 男學生說系統顯示心理學課程額滿，想辦法完成註冊。

questions.q: What will the man most likely do next?
options: Join the waitlist for the first section
Quit his part-time job
Enroll in the morning section
Choose a different subject
questions.why: 男學生最後同意讓職員把他登記到早上的班級。

conversation.setting: A student asks an advisor about adding a class after the add-drop deadline.

turns.text: Excuse me, I wanted to add the statistics course, but I just realized the add-drop period ended last Friday. Am I too late?
Technically, yes, the online system is closed. But you can still add a course with a late-add form if the instructor signs off on it.
Really? That's a relief. Where do I get that form?
You can download it from the department website. Once the professor signs it, bring it back here and I'll process it manually.
Great. Do you know if Professor Lin usually approves these late requests?
She's generally flexible, especially in the first two weeks. But I'd email her today rather than waiting, since her class fills up fast.
I'll email her right after I leave. Thank you so much for explaining the process.

questions.q: What does the man say the woman needs in order to add the course?
options: A payment receipt
A signed late-add form
A doctor's note
A new student ID
questions.why: 顧問說過了退選期仍可用需教授簽名的補加表加課。

questions.q: What does the man advise the woman to do today?
options: Email the professor
Pay a late fee
Retake statistics
Visit the registrar
questions.why: 顧問建議她今天就寄電子郵件給林教授，不要再等。

conversation.setting: A student visits the financial aid office to ask about a merit scholarship.

turns.text: Hi, I received an email saying I might qualify for the merit scholarship, but I'm not sure what documents I need to submit.
Congratulations on qualifying. For the merit scholarship, you'll need your latest transcript and two letters of recommendation from faculty.
I have my transcript, but two recommendation letters? The deadline is in a week. That's a bit tight.
I understand. Most professors are used to writing these, but a week is short notice. I'd ask instructors who know your work well.
Makes sense. Can the letters be emailed directly, or do they have to be printed?
They should be emailed directly to our office from the professor's official university account. That way we can verify they're authentic.
Got it. I'll reach out to my two favorite professors this afternoon.

questions.q: What does the woman say the man needs besides his transcript?
options: A personal essay
Two recommendation letters
Proof of income
A photo ID
questions.why: 職員說優秀獎學金除成績單外還需兩封教授推薦信。

questions.q: How must the recommendation letters be submitted?
options: Mailed as printed copies
Handed in by the student
Emailed from the professor's university account
Uploaded to a public website
questions.why: 職員說推薦信須由教授的官方校內信箱直接寄出以便驗證。

conversation.setting: A student visits a professor during office hours to discuss an essay grade.

turns.text: Professor Adams, thanks for seeing me. I wanted to talk about my essay. I was surprised to get a B-minus.
I'm glad you came in. Your argument was interesting, but the essay lacked evidence in the middle section. You made claims without citing sources.
I see. I did feel rushed near the end. So the problem was mainly the lack of supporting evidence?
Exactly. Your introduction and conclusion were strong. If you strengthen the body paragraphs, your writing will improve a lot.
Would it be possible to revise the paper for a higher grade?
I don't usually allow rewrites for a grade change, but I'll give you detailed comments so you can apply them to your next assignment.
That would be really helpful. I'd rather learn how to fix it than just chase points.

questions.q: What was the main weakness of the woman's essay?
options: A weak introduction
Poor grammar
Insufficient supporting evidence
An unclear conclusion
questions.why: 教授指出文章中段缺乏證據，只提主張卻未引用來源。

questions.q: What does the professor offer to do?
options: Change the grade immediately
Provide detailed comments for future work
Let her rewrite the paper for points
Excuse her from the next assignment
questions.why: 教授說通常不接受重寫改分，但會給詳細評語供下次作業參考。

conversation.setting: Two classmates discuss how to divide the work for a group presentation.

turns.text: So we've been paired up for the marketing presentation. Have you thought about how we should split the work?
A little. There are basically three parts: the research, the slides, and the actual talking. Do you have a preference?
Honestly, I'm terrible at public speaking, but I'm good with design. Would you mind handling most of the presenting if I make the slides?
That works for me. I actually enjoy presenting. How about we both do the research so the content is balanced?
Perfect. Let's each find three sources by Wednesday and then meet to combine everything.
Sounds good. I'll set up a shared folder so we can upload our sources as we go.
Great idea. I'll start on a slide template tonight so we have something to build on.

questions.q: What will the man be mainly responsible for?
options: Presenting the talk
Making the slides
Booking a room
Grading the project
questions.why: 男生說自己不擅長演講但擅長設計，負責製作投影片。

questions.q: What does the woman say she will do?
options: Set up a shared folder
Present alone
Email the professor
Write the whole script
questions.why: 女生說她會建立共享資料夾以便上傳資料。

conversation.setting: A student asks a professor about a scheduling conflict between two final exams.

turns.text: Professor Reyes, I just noticed that my chemistry final and my history final are scheduled at the exact same time. What should I do?
Ah, an exam conflict. That happens occasionally. University policy says the course with the higher number gets rescheduled. Which numbers are they?
Chemistry is 201 and history is 305.
Then the history exam is the one to move, since it's the higher number. You'll need to arrange a make-up time with your history professor.
Okay. Do I need any special form, or do I just talk to the history professor directly?
You should fill out an exam conflict form from the registrar first, then bring it to your history professor as proof.
Understood. I'll pick up the form tomorrow morning before class.

questions.q: According to the professor, which exam should be rescheduled?
options: The chemistry exam
The history exam
Both exams
Neither exam
questions.why: 教授說依規定課號較高者改期，歷史305高於化學201。

questions.q: What does the professor tell the woman to do first?
options: Email both professors
Skip one exam
Get an exam conflict form from the registrar
Retake chemistry
questions.why: 教授說先到註冊組領考試衝突表，再拿去給歷史教授。

conversation.setting: A student speaks with an advisor about withdrawing from an economics course.

turns.text: Hi, I'm thinking about dropping my economics class. It's just too much on top of my other five courses.
Let's look at your options. The deadline to drop without a W on your transcript was two weeks ago, so a withdrawal now would show up as a W.
A W? Will that hurt my chances of getting into grad school?
One W rarely matters to admissions committees. What concerns me more is that dropping below twelve credits could affect your full-time status and your financial aid.
Oh, I didn't think about that. How many credits would I have left?
You'd drop from fifteen to twelve, so you'd stay exactly at the full-time minimum. You'd be fine, but there's no room to drop anything else.
That's cutting it close. Let me think it over tonight before I decide.

questions.q: What is the advisor's main concern about the man dropping the class?
options: His grade point average
The W on his transcript
His graduation date
His full-time status and financial aid
questions.why: 顧問最擔心學分降到十二以下會影響全職身分與助學金。

questions.q: What will the man do next?
options: Drop the class immediately
Add another course
Think it over before deciding
Apply for grad school
questions.why: 男生說今晚再考慮一下才決定。

conversation.setting: A student meets an advisor to discuss changing her major.

turns.text: Thanks for meeting me. I've been thinking about switching my major from biology to environmental science.
That's a fairly common switch, and the two share several core courses. What's motivating the change?
I realized I'm much more interested in ecosystems and policy than in lab work. Biology feels too focused on cellular stuff for me.
That makes sense. The good news is that most of your biology credits will transfer, so you probably won't lose much time.
Really? I was worried I'd have to start over and graduate late.
Not at all. You'd need maybe two extra courses. Let me print out a comparison of the two degree plans so you can see the overlap.
That would be wonderful. Seeing it on paper will make the decision much easier.

questions.q: Why does the woman want to change her major?
options: She is failing biology
She prefers ecosystems and policy over lab work
Her advisor recommended it
Environmental science is easier
questions.why: 女生說她對生態系與政策更有興趣，不喜歡生物偏重細胞的內容。

questions.q: What will the advisor do next?
options: Print a comparison of the two degree plans
Deny her request
Sign her up for lab work
Delay her graduation
questions.why: 顧問說會印出兩種學位計畫的比較讓她參考。

conversation.setting: A student asks a professor for an extension on a research paper.

turns.text: Professor Nguyen, I wanted to ask if I could have a couple extra days on the research paper due Friday.
I'm willing to consider it. Can you tell me what's going on?
I've been dealing with a bad case of the flu all week, and I have a doctor's note if you need it. I've barely been able to sit at my desk.
I'm sorry to hear that. Health comes first. I can give you until Monday, but I can't extend it beyond that because grades are due.
Monday would be perfect. Thank you so much for understanding.
Of course. Just email me the note so I have a record, and focus on getting better this weekend.
I will. I really appreciate your flexibility.

questions.q: Why does the man visit the professor?
options: To ask for an extension on a paper
To drop the course
To dispute a grade
To join a study group
questions.why: 男生請求研究報告延後幾天再交。

questions.q: Why can't the professor extend the deadline beyond Monday?
options: She is going on vacation
Grades are due
The man missed too many classes
The library is closed
questions.why: 教授說因為要交成績，最多只能延到週一。

conversation.setting: A student requests an official transcript at the registrar's office.

turns.text: Hi, I need to request an official transcript to send to a graduate program. How do I do that?
You can order it through the student portal under Records. Do you need it mailed or sent electronically?
Electronically, if possible. The application portal accepts digital transcripts.
Electronic delivery is faster and cheaper, so that's a good choice. There's a five-dollar fee per copy, charged to your student account.
That's fine. How long does electronic delivery usually take?
Normally within one business day, but during application season it can take up to three days, so order early.
Good to know. I'll submit the request tonight so it arrives on time.

questions.q: How does the woman want her transcript delivered?
options: By mail
Picked up in person
Electronically
By fax
questions.why: 女生說希望以電子方式寄送，因為申請系統接受數位成績單。

questions.q: How long can electronic delivery take during application season?
options: One hour
One week
One business day
Up to three days
questions.why: 職員說申請旺季電子寄送最多可能要三天，建議提早申請。

conversation.setting: A student asks a professor to waive a prerequisite for an advanced seminar.

turns.text: Professor Cho, I'd like to take your advanced statistics seminar, but the system blocked me because I haven't taken Statistics 200.
I see. Statistics 200 is listed as a prerequisite for a reason. What background do you have?
I took two years of statistics in high school and an online college-level course last summer. I think I'm prepared for the material.
That online course might be equivalent. Do you have documentation, like a syllabus or a certificate?
Yes, I have both saved. I can forward them to you today.
Please do. If the content matches, I'll sign a prerequisite waiver so you can register. But I'll expect you to keep up if you join.
Absolutely. I'm ready for the challenge. I'll send everything within the hour.

questions.q: Why is the man unable to register for the seminar?
options: The class is full
He hasn't taken a required prerequisite
He missed the deadline
He owes tuition
questions.why: 系統因他未修統計200這門先修課而擋住註冊。

questions.q: What will the man do next?
options: Retake high school statistics
Drop the seminar
Send the professor his course documentation
Pay a registration fee
questions.why: 男生說會在一小時內把大綱與證書寄給教授。

conversation.setting: A student asks a study abroad advisor about a semester in Madrid.

turns.text: Hi, I'm interested in the study abroad program in Madrid for next spring. Is it too late to apply?
You're actually right on time. The application closes at the end of this month. Have you met the language requirement?
I've completed three semesters of Spanish. Is that enough?
The Madrid program requires four semesters or the equivalent, so you're one short. However, you could take an intensive summer course to qualify.
An intensive summer course? That could work. Would the credits count toward my degree too?
They would count as elective credits, yes. I'd recommend applying now and listing the summer course as in progress on your form.
That's a smart approach. I'll start the application this week.

questions.q: What requirement does the woman not yet meet?
options: The GPA requirement
The language requirement
The payment deadline
The credit minimum
questions.why: 女生只修三學期西班牙文，馬德里計畫要求四學期，尚差一學期。

questions.q: How can the woman still qualify for the program?
options: By taking an intensive summer course
By switching to another country
By waiting a year
By dropping Spanish
questions.why: 顧問建議她上暑期密集班補足語言要求。

conversation.setting: A student asks a librarian for help finding sources for a history paper.

turns.text: Excuse me, I'm writing a paper on medieval trade routes, but I can only find general encyclopedia articles. I need scholarly sources.
For scholarly material, you'll want our academic databases rather than the open web. Have you tried JSTOR or the history database?
I've heard of JSTOR but never used it. Is it hard to navigate?
Not at all. You log in with your student ID, then search by keyword. I can show you how to filter results to peer-reviewed articles only.
That would be great. My professor specifically wants peer-reviewed sources.
Then let's start there. Come over to this computer and I'll walk you through a sample search on medieval trade.
Thank you. This is exactly the kind of help I needed.

questions.q: What kind of sources does the man need for his paper?
options: Encyclopedia articles
Newspaper clippings
Blog posts
Peer-reviewed scholarly articles
questions.why: 男生說教授指定要同儕審查的學術來源，而非百科文章。

questions.q: What will the woman do next?
options: Walk him through a sample database search
Write the paper for him
Email his professor
Order a book
questions.why: 館員說到電腦前示範一次中世紀貿易的資料庫搜尋。

conversation.setting: A student tries to schedule tutoring but is in the wrong center.

turns.text: Hi, I'd like to book a tutoring session for my calculus class. I'm really struggling with derivatives.
I can point you in the right direction. Actually, this is the writing center, but the math tutoring center is one floor up in room 210.
Oh, I'm in the wrong place. Sorry about that. Do I need to make an appointment there, or can I just walk in?
During midterms, walk-ins fill up fast, so I'd book ahead. You can reserve a slot on the tutoring website under Math.
Got it. Do you know if they have evening sessions? I have classes all day.
They do, until eight p.m. on weekdays. Evening slots are popular, so grab one as soon as you can.
Perfect. I'll go upstairs and check their schedule right now.

questions.q: What is the woman struggling with?
options: Writing essays
Spanish grammar
Reading comprehension
Calculus derivatives
questions.why: 女生說她微積分的導數學得很吃力。

questions.q: What does the man recommend she do during midterms?
options: Skip the tutoring center
Only use walk-ins
Book an appointment ahead of time
Study alone
questions.why: 男職員說期中考期間臨時報到很快額滿，建議提前預約。

conversation.setting: A student discusses a possible senior thesis topic with a professor.

turns.text: Professor Bell, I wanted to run my senior thesis idea by you. I'm thinking of studying how social media affects teenage sleep.
That's a timely topic. My only concern is that it's quite broad. A one-year thesis needs a narrower focus to be manageable.
That's fair. How would you suggest I narrow it down?
You could focus on one platform, one specific age group, or one measurable outcome like sleep duration. Pick one angle and go deep.
I like the idea of focusing on sleep duration in high school students. That feels concrete.
That's much stronger. Before our next meeting, write a one-page proposal with three research questions.
I'll have that ready by Thursday. Thanks for helping me sharpen the idea.

questions.q: What is the professor's main concern about the man's topic?
options: It is too broad
It is not original
It has been done before
It is too difficult
questions.why: 教授說題目太廣，一年的論文需要更聚焦才好處理。

questions.q: What will the man do before the next meeting?
options: Choose a new topic
Interview students
Read ten books
Write a one-page proposal with three research questions
questions.why: 教授要他下次見面前寫一頁含三個研究問題的提案。

conversation.setting: A student asks the financial aid office how work-study jobs work.

turns.text: Hi, my financial aid package mentions work-study, but I don't know how to actually find a job. Can you explain?
Sure. Work-study means the government funds part of your wages, which makes you an attractive hire for campus departments.
So do you assign me a job, or do I apply for one myself?
You apply yourself. There's a job board on the student portal listing all the work-study openings, from library desks to lab assistants.
That's more freedom than I expected. Is there a limit on how many hours I can work?
Yes, you're capped at twenty hours a week during the semester, so it won't interfere with your classes.
That sounds manageable. I'll browse the job board tonight.

questions.q: How does the man say the woman should find a work-study job?
options: By applying herself through the job board
By waiting for an assignment
By asking her professors
By visiting each department in person
questions.why: 職員說她要自己上學生入口網的職缺看板申請。

questions.q: What is the limit on work-study hours during the semester?
options: Ten hours a week
Twenty hours a week
Thirty hours a week
Forty hours a week
questions.why: 職員說學期間每週工時上限為二十小時，不會影響上課。

conversation.setting: A student asks an advisor about taking more than the maximum number of credits.

turns.text: Hi, I want to take six courses next semester, but the system won't let me register for more than five. Can I get an override?
Taking eighteen or more credits requires a course overload petition. It's approved based on your academic record. What's your current GPA?
It's a 3.8. I've never dropped a class or gotten below a B.
With a 3.8, you clearly meet the requirement. Overloads are usually approved for students above a 3.5.
That's a relief. What do I need to do to petition?
Fill out the overload form, attach an unofficial transcript, and get your academic advisor's signature. Then submit it to the dean's office.
Got it. I'll gather everything and get the signature this week.

questions.q: Why can't the man register for six courses?
options: His GPA is too low
He owes fees
The system caps him at five courses without a petition
The classes conflict
questions.why: 系統限制最多五門，超修十八學分以上需提出超修申請。

questions.q: What does the man need to submit with the overload form?
options: A payment receipt
A doctor's note
Two recommendation letters
An unofficial transcript and his advisor's signature
questions.why: 顧問說要附非正式成績單並取得學術顧問簽名再送院長辦公室。

conversation.setting: A student talks with a professor about receiving an incomplete grade after surgery.

turns.text: Professor Hall, because of my surgery last month, I couldn't finish the final project. Is there any option besides failing?
There is. Given your medical situation, I can assign an incomplete instead of a failing grade. That gives you time to finish.
That would save me. How much time would I have?
University policy allows up to one year, but I'd prefer you finish within the first month of next semester so it doesn't linger.
One month is very doable now that I've recovered. What happens if I don't finish in time?
If the work isn't submitted, the incomplete automatically converts to an F. So treat that deadline seriously.
Understood. I'll aim to submit it within three weeks. Thank you for the second chance.

questions.q: What does the professor offer the woman instead of a failing grade?
options: Extra credit
A retake of the course
A passing grade
An incomplete grade
questions.why: 教授因她的醫療情況，願給未完成成績而非不及格。

questions.q: What happens if the woman does not finish the work in time?
options: The incomplete becomes an F
She gets an extension
The grade stays incomplete
She must retake the course
questions.why: 教授說若未按時繳交，未完成成績會自動轉為不及格。

conversation.setting: A student asks a clerk about registering for a required lab section.

turns.text: Hi, I registered for the biology lecture, but I'm confused. Do I also have to sign up for a lab separately?
Yes, the lecture and lab are listed as separate registrations, even though they're one course. Many students miss the lab and get dropped.
Oh no, I almost forgot. Which lab sections still have space?
Let me check. The Monday afternoon and Friday morning labs are open. The Wednesday one is already full.
Friday morning works best with my schedule. Can you add me to that one?
Absolutely. But remember, you must attend the first lab session, or your spot gets released to someone on the waitlist.
I'll definitely be there. Thanks for catching that before it became a problem.

questions.q: Why is the man confused?
options: He failed the lecture
He lost his ID
He didn't know the lab required separate registration
He wants to drop the course
questions.why: 男生不知道講座與實驗課雖屬同一門課卻要分開註冊。

questions.q: What must the man do to keep his lab spot?
options: Pay a lab fee
Email the professor
Switch to Wednesday
Attend the first lab session
questions.why: 職員說必須出席第一堂實驗課，否則名額會讓給候補者。

conversation.setting: Two classmates plan the timing of their joint class presentation.

turns.text: Our presentation is twenty minutes total, and we each need to talk. How should we split the time?
Since there are two of us, ten minutes each seems fair. Do you want to take the first half or the second?
I'll take the first half with the introduction and background. You could handle the case studies and conclusion.
That works. Should we build in time for questions at the end?
Good point. Let's plan for eight minutes each of talking, and save four minutes for questions.
Smart. Let's do a practice run on Sunday to check our timing.
Agreed. If we run over, we can trim slides before the real thing on Monday.

questions.q: How will the two speakers divide the presentation?
options: Each speaks for about half the time
The man does all the talking
The woman skips her part
They present on different days
questions.why: 兩人決定各講一半時間，女生講前半、男生講後半。

questions.q: What do the speakers decide to do on Sunday?
options: Email the professor
Cancel the presentation
Write new slides
Do a practice run to check timing
questions.why: 男生提議週日先演練一次以確認時間掌控。

conversation.setting: A student asks an advisor how to appeal a final course grade.

turns.text: Hi, I got a C in my literature course, but I calculated my scores and I think it should be a B. How do I appeal?
Grade appeals are possible, but the first step is always to talk directly with the professor. Have you done that yet?
Not yet. I wasn't sure if I was allowed to just email them about a grade.
You absolutely can, and you should. Most grade discrepancies turn out to be simple math errors that the professor can fix quickly.
That makes sense. What if the professor and I still disagree after talking?
Then you can file a formal appeal with the department chair within thirty days of receiving the grade. But start with the professor.
Okay, I'll email the professor today and go from there.

questions.q: What is the first step in appealing a grade?
options: Filing a formal complaint
Contacting the dean
Retaking the course
Talking directly with the professor
questions.why: 顧問說申訴成績的第一步一定是先直接找教授談。

questions.q: According to the advisor, what causes most grade discrepancies?
options: Cheating
Late assignments
Simple math errors
Missing exams
questions.why: 顧問說多數成績出入其實是簡單的計算錯誤，教授能很快更正。

conversation.setting: A student asks an advisor whether a summer internship can count for course credit.

turns.text: Hi, I've been offered a summer internship at a marketing firm. Can I earn course credit for it?
Possibly. To count for credit, the internship must relate to your major and be supervised by a faculty sponsor. What's your major?
Marketing, so it's a direct match. How do I find a faculty sponsor?
Usually a professor in your department whose interests align with the work. Professor Diaz supervises most marketing internships.
I'll reach out to her. Is there a minimum number of hours I need to work?
Yes, you need at least a hundred and twenty hours over the summer, plus a final reflection paper, to earn three credits.
That's very doable. I'll contact Professor Diaz this week to get started.

questions.q: What must be true for the internship to count for credit?
options: It must be paid
It must relate to her major and have a faculty sponsor
It must be off campus
It must last two years
questions.why: 顧問說實習須與主修相關並由教職員擔任指導者才能計學分。

questions.q: What does the woman need to do to earn three credits?
options: Pay a fee
Take an exam
Present to the class
Work 120 hours and write a reflection paper
questions.why: 顧問說暑期需至少工作一百二十小時並繳交心得報告才能得三學分。

conversation.setting: A student asks a department clerk about a waitlist for a popular seminar.

turns.text: Hi, I really want to get into the film studies seminar, but it's full. Is there a waitlist I can join?
There is, and you're number four right now. Seminars like this often have people drop in the first week, so your odds are decent.
That's encouraging. Do I need to do anything to hold my spot on the waitlist?
Just keep attending the class as if you're enrolled. Professors usually admit waitlisted students who show up consistently.
So going to class actually helps my chances?
Definitely. If a seat opens and the professor sees you've been there every session, you'll likely be the one they add.
Then I'll be at every class from day one. Thanks for the tip.

questions.q: What is the man's position on the waitlist?
options: Number four
Number one
Number ten
Number twenty
questions.why: 職員說他目前排在候補名單第四位。

questions.q: How can the man improve his chances of getting into the seminar?
options: By paying extra
By emailing the dean
By attending every class consistently
By switching majors
questions.why: 職員說教授通常會錄取持續到課的候補學生。

conversation.setting: A senior meets an advisor to confirm she has met all graduation requirements.

turns.text: Hi, I'm graduating in the spring and I want to make sure I've met all my requirements. Can we do a degree check?
Of course, that's exactly what seniors should do. Let me pull up your records. You've completed your major and all general education courses.
That's a relief. So I'm all set?
Almost. It looks like you're still missing one requirement: the two-course foreign language sequence. You've only completed the first course.
Oh, I completely forgot about the second language course. Can I take it in the spring?
Yes, and it's offered next semester, so the timing works perfectly. Register for it as soon as your enrollment window opens.
I'll do that the moment registration opens. Thank you for catching this.

questions.q: What requirement is the woman still missing?
options: Her major courses
General education courses
Her final exams
The second foreign language course
questions.why: 顧問發現她只修了外語序列的第一門，還差第二門課。

questions.q: What will the woman do next?
options: Register for the missing course when enrollment opens
Delay her graduation a year
Change her major
Retake general education courses
questions.why: 女生說一開放註冊就立刻選那門缺的課。

conversation.setting: A man calls a dental clinic to schedule an appointment.

turns.text: Hi, I'd like to book a cleaning with Dr. Patel. Do you have anything next week?
Let me see. Dr. Patel is fully booked next week, but she has an opening the following Monday at nine.
Monday morning is tricky for me. Is there any chance of an afternoon slot?
There's a two-thirty on that same Monday, if that works better for you.
Two-thirty is perfect. Oh, and it's been about a year, so I might need X-rays too, right?
Most likely, yes. I'll block out a little extra time. Can I get your name and date of birth?
Sure, it's Daniel Ortiz, born March fourth, nineteen ninety-five.

questions.q: What is the man mainly trying to do?
options: Cancel an appointment with Dr. Patel.
Schedule a dental cleaning.
Complain about a long wait time.
Ask for the results of his X-rays.
questions.why: 男子打電話的主要目的是預約洗牙。

questions.q: What time does the man finally agree to?
options: Next Monday at nine in the morning.
Any afternoon slot next week.
The following Monday at nine.
The following Monday at two-thirty.
questions.why: 他最後選了隔週週一下午兩點半的時段。

conversation.setting: A woman picks up a prescription at a pharmacy counter.

turns.text: Hi, I'm here to pick up a prescription for Emily Chen.
Let me find it. Here we are. This is the antibiotic your doctor sent over. Have you taken this one before?
No, this is my first time. Is there anything I should watch out for?
Take one capsule twice a day with food, and be sure to finish the whole course even if you feel better.
Got it. Can I take it together with my allergy medicine?
That's fine, but I'd space them out by a couple of hours. And avoid alcohol while you're on it.
Okay, that's good to know. How much do I owe you?
With your insurance, it comes to twelve dollars.

questions.q: What does the pharmacist warn the woman to do?
options: Finish all the medicine even if she feels better.
Take the capsule only when she feels sick.
Stop her allergy medicine immediately.
Take the antibiotic on an empty stomach.
questions.why: 藥師提醒她即使症狀好轉,也要把整個療程吃完。

questions.q: What does the pharmacist say about her allergy medicine?
options: She must stop taking it completely.
It cannot be taken with the antibiotic.
She should space the two out by a couple of hours.
She needs a new prescription for it.
questions.why: 藥師說兩種藥可以一起吃,但要間隔幾個小時。

conversation.setting: A man asks a gym staff member about membership options.

turns.text: Hi, I'm thinking about joining. What kind of memberships do you offer?
We have a monthly plan and an annual one. The annual works out cheaper if you plan to stay a while.
I'm new in town, so I'm not sure how long I'll be here. Can I try the place out first?
Of course. We offer a free three-day pass so you can test the equipment and classes.
That sounds great. Do the group classes cost extra?
Classes like yoga and spin are included, but personal training sessions are billed separately.
Perfect. I think I'll just start with the free pass today, then.
Great choice. Let me get you to fill out this quick form.

questions.q: Why is the man hesitant to buy the annual membership?
options: He thinks the gym is too expensive.
He dislikes the group classes.
He already has another membership.
He isn't sure how long he'll stay in town.
questions.why: 他剛搬來,不確定會待多久,所以還不想買年費方案。

questions.q: What will the man most likely do next?
options: Sign up for the annual plan.
Fill out a form for the free pass.
Book a personal training session.
Join a yoga class right away.
questions.why: 他決定先用免費三日券,店員請他填寫表格。

conversation.setting: A woman speaks with an airline agent at the check-in counter.

turns.text: Hi, I'm checking in for the two o'clock flight to Chicago. Here's my passport.
Thank you. You're all set. Are you checking any bags today?
Just one. Oh, but I'm a little worried it might be overweight.
Let's put it on the scale. Hmm, it's twenty-four kilos, and the limit is twenty-three, so it's just over.
Oh no. Do I have to pay a fee?
You can, or you could move a few items into your carry-on to bring it under the limit and save the charge.
That makes sense. Let me shift a couple of things around.
No rush. I'll wait right here while you sort it out.

questions.q: What problem does the woman have?
options: She has lost her passport.
Her flight has been delayed.
Her checked bag is slightly overweight.
She missed her connecting flight.
questions.why: 她要托運的行李超重了一公斤。

questions.q: What does the agent suggest she do?
options: Move some items into her carry-on.
Pay the overweight fee right away.
Leave some belongings behind.
Take a later flight to Chicago.
questions.why: 服務員建議她把一些東西移到隨身行李,以避免超重費。

conversation.setting: A man checks in at a hotel front desk.

turns.text: Good afternoon. I have a reservation under the name Reynolds, for three nights.
Welcome. I've found your booking. I see you requested a room with a city view.
That's right. Is it possible to get one on a higher floor?
Let me check. Yes, I can offer you room twelve-oh-two on the twelfth floor. It has a lovely view.
Wonderful. What time is breakfast served?
Breakfast is from six-thirty to ten in the second-floor restaurant. Here's your key card.
Thanks. Oh, and is the gym open twenty-four hours?
It is, and it's on the ground floor, just next to the pool.

questions.q: What special request had the man made with his reservation?
options: A room close to the elevator.
A room with a city view.
An extra bed for a child.
A late checkout time.
questions.why: 他訂房時要求要一間有城市景觀的房間。

questions.q: Where is the gym located?
options: On the twelfth floor next to his room.
On the second floor by the restaurant.
It is currently closed for repairs.
On the ground floor near the pool.
questions.why: 櫃檯說健身房在一樓,就在泳池旁邊。

conversation.setting: A woman returns a rental car to the counter.

turns.text: Hi, I'm returning this car. It's the blue sedan in space fourteen.
Thank you. Did you refill the tank before bringing it back?
I did, right at the station down the road. Here's the receipt.
Great, that saves you the refueling charge. Did you have any trouble with the vehicle?
It ran perfectly. Although I did notice a small scratch on the rear bumper when I picked it up.
I'm glad you mentioned it. Let me check our notes... yes, that was already recorded, so you won't be charged.
Oh good, I was a little worried about that.
No need. Everything looks fine. I'll email your final receipt within the hour.

questions.q: Why does the woman mention the scratch on the bumper?
options: She wants to make sure she isn't blamed for it.
She caused it and wants to pay for it.
She wants a discount on her next rental.
She thinks the car is unsafe to drive.
questions.why: 她提起刮痕是想確認那不是她造成的,以免被收費。

questions.q: What does the agent say he will do next?
options: Charge her a refueling fee.
Inspect the car's engine.
Email her the final receipt within an hour.
Give her a different car.
questions.why: 服務員說會在一小時內把最終收據寄到她的信箱。

conversation.setting: A tourist asks a local how to get to the train station.

turns.text: Excuse me, could you tell me how to get to the central train station from here?
Sure. It's about a fifteen-minute walk, or you could take the number six bus right across the street.
I've got a big suitcase, so the bus might be easier. Where do I get off?
Take it three stops and get off at Market Square. The station is right behind it, you can't miss it.
Great. Do you know how often the bus comes?
Usually every ten minutes or so. There's one due any minute now, actually.
Then I'd better hurry. Thank you so much for your help!
No problem. Have a safe trip!

questions.q: Why does the man decide to take the bus instead of walking?
options: The walk is too far to manage.
It is starting to rain heavily.
He doesn't know the way on foot.
He is carrying a large suitcase.
questions.why: 他帶著一個大行李箱,覺得搭公車比較輕鬆。

questions.q: Where should the man get off the bus?
options: At the central train station entrance.
At Market Square, three stops away.
At the sixth stop on the route.
Right across the street from here.
questions.why: 當地人說搭三站在市場廣場下車,車站就在後面。

conversation.setting: A woman calls to reschedule a doctor's appointment.

turns.text: Hi, I have an appointment with Dr. Lee this Thursday, but something's come up at work. Can I move it?
Of course. Let me pull up your file. What day would suit you better?
Would early next week be possible? Monday or Tuesday, ideally in the morning.
Dr. Lee has a slot Tuesday at ten-fifteen. How does that sound?
Tuesday at ten-fifteen is perfect. Should I still fast beforehand for the blood test?
Yes, please don't eat anything after midnight the night before. Water is fine, though.
Understood. Thanks for being so flexible.
You're welcome. I'll send you a reminder text the day before.

questions.q: Why is the woman calling the clinic?
options: To cancel her blood test.
To ask for her test results.
To reschedule her appointment.
To find a different doctor.
questions.why: 她因為工作臨時有事,想把週四的門診改期。

questions.q: What must the woman do before her appointment?
options: Avoid eating after midnight the night before.
Drink no water on the morning of the test.
Bring a copy of her insurance card.
Arrive an hour early to fill out forms.
questions.why: 因為要抽血,她前一晚午夜後不能進食,但可以喝水。

conversation.setting: A man asks a pharmacist for help choosing a cold remedy.

turns.text: Hi, I've got a stuffy nose and a cough. Can you recommend something over the counter?
Sure. Is the cough dry, or are you bringing anything up?
It's pretty dry, and honestly it's keeping me awake at night.
In that case, this nighttime syrup should help you rest. It relieves the cough and clears congestion.
Does it make you drowsy? I have to drive to work in the mornings.
It does, so only take it before bed. For the daytime, this non-drowsy tablet is a better choice.
That's helpful. I'll take both, then.
Good idea. Just don't use them at the same time.

questions.q: What is the man's main concern about the syrup?
options: That it is too expensive.
That it might make him drowsy while driving.
That it tastes unpleasant.
That it won't help his cough.
questions.why: 他早上要開車上班,擔心糖漿會讓他想睡。

questions.q: What does the pharmacist recommend for the daytime?
options: The nighttime syrup taken twice.
Drinking plenty of warm water only.
Skipping medicine until the evening.
A non-drowsy tablet.
questions.why: 藥師建議白天改用不會使人想睡的藥錠。

conversation.setting: A woman signs up for a fitness class at a gym.

turns.text: Hi, I'd like to join the beginners' spin class. Is there any space this week?
Let me check the schedule. The Wednesday evening class is full, but Saturday morning has a few spots.
Saturday could work. I've never done spin before, though. Is it very intense?
The beginners' class is gentle, and I'll show you how to adjust the bike so it's comfortable.
That's reassuring. Do I need to bring anything special?
Just water and a towel. We provide the bikes and everything else. Wear comfortable clothes.
Perfect. Sign me up for Saturday, then.
Done. See you at nine on Saturday morning.

questions.q: Why can't the woman take the Wednesday class?
options: It is already full.
It is only for advanced riders.
It costs more than she expected.
It is at an inconvenient time.
questions.why: 週三晚上的課程已經額滿了。

questions.q: What does the instructor tell her to bring?
options: Her own spin bike.
A pair of cycling shoes.
Water and a towel.
A signed medical form.
questions.why: 教練說只要帶水和毛巾,其他器材都由健身房提供。

conversation.setting: A man asks an airport information desk about a delayed flight.

turns.text: Excuse me, my flight to Denver was supposed to leave at noon, but the board says delayed. Do you know why?
Let me look it up. Yes, flight two-oh-seven is delayed due to weather. The new departure time is two-thirty.
Two-thirty? I have a connection in Denver at four. Will I make it?
It'll be tight. I'd recommend speaking to your airline about rebooking the connection, just to be safe.
Good idea. Where's the airline desk?
It's near gate B12, just past the coffee shop on your left.
Thank you. I'll head there right now.
You're welcome. Better to sort it out early.

questions.q: Why is the man's flight delayed?
options: A mechanical problem with the plane.
A missing crew member.
A security issue at the gate.
Bad weather.
questions.why: 服務員說班機因為天氣因素而延誤。

questions.q: What does the staff member advise the man to do?
options: Wait at the gate for an announcement.
Talk to his airline about the connection.
Buy a ticket for a different flight.
File a complaint about the delay.
questions.why: 她建議他去找航空公司,重新安排轉機以防萬一。

conversation.setting: A woman calls the hotel front desk about a problem in her room.

turns.text: Hi, this is room five-fourteen. The air conditioning in my room doesn't seem to be working.
I'm sorry about that. Is it not turning on at all, or is it just not cooling?
It turns on, but it's only blowing warm air. The room is getting pretty stuffy.
I apologize for the trouble. I can send someone up to look at it within fifteen minutes.
That would be great. I'm heading out to dinner soon, though.
No problem. Our technician can enter with a master key while you're out, if that's all right.
Yes, that's fine. Please just leave a note when it's fixed.
Absolutely. And if it can't be repaired tonight, we'll move you to another room.

questions.q: What is wrong with the woman's room?
options: The heating is broken.
The window won't close.
The air conditioning only blows warm air.
The room has not been cleaned.
questions.why: 冷氣雖然能開,但只吹出暖風,房間變得很悶。

questions.q: What will happen if the problem cannot be fixed tonight?
options: The woman will be moved to another room.
The woman will get a full refund.
The technician will come back tomorrow.
The woman will have to check out early.
questions.why: 櫃檯說如果今晚修不好,就會幫她換到另一個房間。

conversation.setting: A man picks up a rental car and discusses insurance.

turns.text: Hi, I have a reservation for a compact car for the weekend, under Johnson.
Found it. Would you like to add our collision insurance? It's twenty dollars a day.
Hmm, my credit card already covers rental insurance, so I think I'll skip it.
That's perfectly fine. Just so you know, without it, any damage would go through your card provider.
Understood. I've done that before, so I'm comfortable with it.
Great. The car is a gray hatchback in spot nine. Please return it with a full tank by Sunday at noon.
Will do. Is there a grace period if I'm a little late?
You get a thirty-minute grace period; after that it's an extra hourly charge.

questions.q: Why does the man decline the collision insurance?
options: He thinks the car is too cheap to insure.
His credit card already provides rental coverage.
He plans to drive very carefully.
He cannot afford the daily fee.
questions.why: 他的信用卡已經包含租車保險,所以不加購。

questions.q: What does the agent say about returning the car late?
options: There is no penalty for a late return.
He must call ahead to extend the rental.
The car will be reported as stolen.
There's a thirty-minute grace period, then an hourly charge.
questions.why: 服務員說有三十分鐘的寬限,超過就會按小時加收費用。

conversation.setting: A woman asks a passerby for directions to a museum.

turns.text: Excuse me, I'm trying to find the modern art museum. Am I going the right way?
Actually, you're headed in the opposite direction. You'll want to turn around and go back two blocks.
Oh, thank goodness I asked. And then?
At the second block, turn left onto Bridge Street. The museum is a big glass building on the corner.
Is it far? My feet are killing me from all the walking.
It's only about five minutes from here. There's also a nice café right next to it if you need a rest.
That sounds perfect. Thank you so much!
