You're welcome. Enjoy the exhibits.

questions.q: What mistake was the woman making?
options: She was walking in the wrong direction.
She had the wrong address written down.
She was looking for the wrong museum.
She had already passed the building.
questions.why: 她原本走的方向剛好相反,必須掉頭往回走。

questions.q: What does the man mention is next to the museum?
options: A subway station.
A large parking lot.
A café where she can rest.
Another art gallery.
questions.why: 男子提到博物館旁邊有間咖啡館,可以休息一下。

conversation.setting: A man checks in at a clinic reception for a physical exam.

turns.text: Hi, I have a nine-thirty appointment with Dr. Nguyen for my annual physical.
Welcome. Can I see your ID and insurance card, please?
Here you go. Oh, I filled out the health forms online last night.
Perfect, I can see them here. That'll save us some time. Have a seat, and the nurse will call you shortly.
Great. Roughly how long is the wait? I have a meeting at eleven.
The doctor's running on schedule today, so no more than ten minutes.
That's a relief. I should make my meeting easily, then.
You should be fine. I'll let the nurse know you're in a bit of a hurry.

questions.q: Why is the man at the clinic?
options: To pick up a prescription.
To get a flu shot.
To discuss his test results.
For his annual physical exam.
questions.why: 他是來做一年一度的健康檢查。

questions.q: What did the man do the night before?
options: He called to confirm the appointment.
He filled out the health forms online.
He picked up his insurance card.
He canceled another meeting.
questions.why: 他前一晚已經在線上填好健康表格。

conversation.setting: A woman asks a pharmacist about a prescription refill.

turns.text: Hi, I need to refill my blood pressure medication, but I think I'm out of refills.
Let me check. Yes, it looks like your last refill was used. I'll need to contact your doctor to authorize a new one.
Oh, how long does that usually take? I only have two pills left.
Usually a day or two. But since you're running low, I can give you a three-day emergency supply to tide you over.
That would be a lifesaver, thank you.
No problem. I'll call your doctor's office this afternoon and text you once the refill is ready.
Perfect. I really appreciate it.
Of course. Take care, and don't skip any doses in the meantime.

questions.q: What is the woman's problem?
options: She lost her medication bottle.
She was given the wrong pills.
She has no refills left on her prescription.
She can't afford her medication.
questions.why: 她的處方箋已經沒有可續領的次數了。

questions.q: What does the pharmacist offer to do?
options: Give her a three-day emergency supply.
Switch her to a cheaper brand.
Refund her for the missing pills.
Tell her to visit the emergency room.
questions.why: 藥師提議先給她三天的緊急用藥,以免中斷服藥。

conversation.setting: A man asks the front desk about freezing his gym membership.

turns.text: Hi, I'm going to be traveling for two months. Can I pause my membership instead of canceling it?
Absolutely. We offer a membership freeze for up to three months. There's a small five-dollar monthly fee to hold your spot.
That's much better than paying the full rate. How do I set it up?
I can do it right now. When would you like the freeze to start?
The first of next month would be ideal. I leave on the third.
Done. It'll automatically reactivate after two months, but you can extend it if your trip runs long.
That's flexible, I like that. Thanks a lot.
My pleasure. Safe travels, and we'll see you when you're back.

questions.q: What does the man want to do?
options: Cancel his membership permanently.
Pause his membership while traveling.
Upgrade to a premium plan.
Get a refund for last month.
questions.why: 他要出差兩個月,想暫停會籍而不是取消。

questions.q: What happens after the two-month freeze ends?
options: His membership is automatically canceled.
He must pay all the missed months.
He has to sign up again from scratch.
His membership reactivates automatically.
questions.why: 店員說兩個月後會籍會自動恢復,若行程延長還可延展。

conversation.setting: A woman goes through airport security screening.

turns.text: Hi, do I need to take my laptop out of my bag?
Yes, please place your laptop in a separate bin. Also remove any liquids and your jacket.
Okay. My liquids are all in this small bag. Is that all right?
That's fine, as long as each container is under a hundred milliliters. Your shoes can stay on today.
Great. Oh, I have a metal water bottle, but it's empty.
An empty bottle is no problem. Just send it through the scanner in a bin as well.
Got it. Anything else?
That's everything. Step through the scanner whenever you're ready.

questions.q: What does the officer ask the woman to do first?
options: Put her laptop in a separate bin.
Take off her shoes.
Throw away her water bottle.
Show her boarding pass again.
questions.why: 安檢人員請她先把筆電放進另一個籃子。

questions.q: What does the officer say about her water bottle?
options: She must throw it away.
She has to fill it before boarding.
An empty bottle is fine to send through.
Metal bottles are not allowed.
questions.why: 官員說空的水瓶沒問題,放進籃子過掃描即可。

conversation.setting: A man asks the hotel concierge for restaurant recommendations.

turns.text: Hi, my wife and I want a nice dinner tonight. Any recommendations nearby?
Certainly. Are you in the mood for anything in particular? We have great seafood and Italian options close by.
Seafood sounds perfect. Somewhere not too fancy, though.
Then I'd suggest The Blue Anchor. It's casual, about a ten-minute walk, and the grilled fish is excellent.
That sounds ideal. Do we need a reservation?
On a Friday night, yes. Would you like me to book a table for you? What time works?
Seven-thirty for two, please.
I'll call them right now and leave the confirmation at your room.

questions.q: What kind of restaurant does the man prefer?
options: A fancy Italian restaurant.
A quiet vegetarian café.
A rooftop bar with live music.
A casual seafood place.
questions.why: 他想找一間不太正式的海鮮餐廳。

questions.q: What does the concierge offer to do?
options: Drive them to the restaurant.
Book a table for them.
Give them a discount coupon.
Order room service instead.
questions.why: 禮賓人員主動提議幫他們訂位。

conversation.setting: A woman calls a car rental company to extend her rental.

turns.text: Hi, I rented a car that's due back tomorrow, but my trip got extended. Can I keep it two more days?
Let me pull up your reservation. Can I have your booking number?
Sure, it's four-four-seven-one-two.
Thank you. Yes, the car is available for two extra days. The rate stays the same, so it'll be an additional ninety dollars.
That works. Do I need to bring the car in to sign anything?
No, I can extend it over the phone right now. You'll get a confirmation email shortly.
Wonderful. Same drop-off location, then?
Yes, same location, just by Thursday at noon instead of Tuesday.

questions.q: Why is the woman calling?
options: To report the car as damaged.
To cancel her reservation.
To extend her rental by two days.
To change the drop-off location.
questions.why: 她的行程延長了,想把租車再多延兩天。

questions.q: What does the woman need to do to extend the rental?
options: Nothing; the agent extends it over the phone.
Bring the car in to sign papers.
Pay a large cancellation fee.
Return the car to a new location.
questions.why: 服務員說可以直接在電話中辦理延期,她不必親自跑一趟。

conversation.setting: A man asks a hotel staff member how to get to the beach.

turns.text: Excuse me, what's the easiest way to get to the beach from the hotel?
The nearest beach is just a ten-minute walk. Head out the main entrance and follow Ocean Avenue straight down.
Is that the one with the boardwalk? I heard there's a nice one around here.
Ah, the boardwalk beach is a bit farther, about a mile. You'd be better off taking the free shuttle for that one.
A shuttle? Where does it leave from?
It picks up right outside the lobby every half hour, on the hour and the half-hour.
Perfect. I think we'll take the shuttle to the boardwalk, then.
Great choice. The next one leaves in about twenty minutes.

questions.q: What is the man mainly asking about?
options: The opening hours of the pool.
How to get to the beach.
Where to rent beach equipment.
The best local seafood restaurant.
questions.why: 他主要在問怎麼從飯店去海灘。

questions.q: How does the man decide to reach the boardwalk beach?
options: By walking down Ocean Avenue.
By renting a bicycle.
By taking a taxi.
By taking the free hotel shuttle.
questions.why: 因為木棧道海灘較遠,他決定搭飯店的免費接駁車。

conversation.setting: A woman brings her son to a pediatric clinic without an appointment.

turns.text: Hi, my son has had an earache since last night. We don't have an appointment, but is there any way he can be seen?
Let me see what we can do. Is he in a lot of pain right now?
He's uncomfortable, and he didn't sleep well, but he's not crying or anything.
Okay. We keep a few same-day slots for situations like this. There's an opening at eleven-forty with Dr. Rivera.
That would be wonderful. It's almost eleven now, so we'll wait.
Perfect. In the meantime, please fill out this form with his symptoms and any medications.
Of course. He isn't taking anything right now.
Good to know. Just note that on the form, and we'll call you when the doctor's ready.

questions.q: Why has the woman brought her son to the clinic?
options: He has had an earache since the night before.
He needs a routine vaccination.
He broke his arm at school.
He needs a form signed for sports.
questions.why: 她的兒子從前一晚開始耳朵痛,所以帶他來看診。

questions.q: What does the receptionist ask the woman to do while they wait?
options: Take her son to the emergency room.
Come back later in the afternoon.
Fill out a form about his symptoms.
Give her son some pain medicine.
questions.why: 櫃檯請她先填寫一張關於症狀和用藥的表格。

conversation.setting: A man asks at the airport lost and found about his missing bag.

turns.text: Hi, I think I left my backpack at the gate area about an hour ago. Has anything been turned in?
Let me check. Can you describe it for me?
It's a black backpack with a red zipper, and there's a laptop inside.
We do have a black backpack that was brought in from gate C7. Does that sound like where you were?
Yes! That's exactly where I was sitting. Thank goodness.
Before I release it, I'll need to see some ID, and I'll ask you to confirm what's inside.
Absolutely. My ID's right here, and there should be a silver laptop and a blue notebook inside.
That matches our record. Here you go, and please double-check that nothing's missing.

questions.q: What is the man trying to do?
options: Check in an extra bag.
Recover a backpack he left behind.
Report a stolen laptop.
Buy a new backpack.
questions.why: 他想找回一小時前遺留在登機口的背包。

questions.q: What does the staff member require before returning the bag?
options: A baggage claim ticket.
A small storage fee.
A police report number.
His ID and confirmation of the contents.
questions.why: 工作人員要他出示證件並說出包內物品,才會歸還。

conversation.setting: A woman checks out of a hotel and questions her bill.

turns.text: Hi, I'd like to check out of room three-oh-eight. Here's my key.
Thank you. Let me print your final bill. Everything looks in order... one moment.
Actually, could you explain this thirty-dollar charge? I don't recognize it.
Let me look. Ah, that's for two items from the mini-bar on your second night.
Oh, I never opened the mini-bar. There must be a mistake.
I apologize. Let me remove that right away. There, the charge is gone and your total is updated.
Thank you. Could I also get a printed receipt for my company?
Of course. I'll print one for you now, and email a copy as well.

questions.q: Why does the woman question her bill?
options: The room rate was higher than quoted.
She was charged for an extra night.
There is a mini-bar charge she doesn't recognize.
A late checkout fee was added.
questions.why: 帳單上有一筆她不認得的迷你吧費用,但她根本沒用過。

questions.q: What does the woman ask for at the end?
options: A printed receipt for her company.
A refund to her credit card.
A later checkout time.
Help carrying her luggage.
questions.why: 她最後要求一張可以給公司報帳的紙本收據。

conversation.setting: Two classmates plan to see a newly released film over the weekend.

turns.text: Have you heard about that new sci-fi movie? It opens this Friday and everyone's been talking about it.
I saw the trailer, and it looks incredible. Are you planning to go?
I was hoping to catch the Saturday afternoon show. Do you want to come with me?
I'd love to, but I already promised to help my sister move on Saturday. Could we make it Sunday instead?
Sunday's fine. There's a four o'clock screening. I'll book the tickets online tonight.
Great. I'll pay you back for my half as soon as you do.

questions.q: What does the man mainly want to do?
options: See a movie together with the woman
Help the woman's sister move
Return some tickets he bought
Film a short movie for class
questions.why: 男子提到新電影並邀請女子星期六一起去看，所以他的主要目的是和女子一起看電影。

questions.q: What will the man do tonight?
options: Watch the trailer one more time
Book the tickets online
Help carry furniture
Meet the woman's sister
questions.why: 男子說今晚會上網訂票，因此他接下來的行動是線上購票。

conversation.setting: A woman invites a friend to a concert, but he worries about the ticket price.

turns.text: My favorite band is playing downtown next month, and tickets just went on sale this morning.
Nice! How much do they cost?
The front seats are eighty dollars, but there are seats near the back for forty.
Eighty is a little too much for me right now. I've been trying to save money.
No problem at all. The forty-dollar seats still have a really good view.
In that case, I'm in. Let's get the cheaper ones.

questions.q: Why does the man hesitate at first?
options: He doesn't like the band
He is busy next month
He thinks the front seats are too expensive
He has already seen the band live
questions.why: 男子說八十美元對現在的他太貴了，因為他在存錢，所以他猶豫是覺得前排座位太貴。

questions.q: What do the speakers decide to do?
options: Skip the concert entirely
Buy the eighty-dollar seats
Wait for a discount
Buy the forty-dollar seats
questions.why: 男子最後說要加入並買便宜的票，因此兩人決定購買四十美元的座位。

conversation.setting: A student urges a friend to join the campus photography club.

turns.text: You're still into photography, right? Those shots you took last semester were great.
Yeah, I love it, but I usually just shoot on my own. Why do you ask?
The photography club is recruiting this week. You should really sign up.
I'm not sure my photos are good enough for a club, to be honest.
Don't worry about that. Half the members are beginners. There's a welcome meeting Thursday at six in room 210.
If it's that relaxed, I'll give it a shot. Save me a seat.

questions.q: What is the man mainly trying to do?
options: Borrow the woman's camera
Encourage the woman to join a club
Sell some of his photographs
Cancel a club meeting
questions.why: 男子告訴女子攝影社正在招募並鼓勵她報名，所以他主要是想讓女子加入社團。

questions.q: Why is the woman hesitant at first?
options: She doubts her photos are good enough
She does not own a camera
She dislikes group activities
She is busy every Thursday
questions.why: 女子說她不確定自己的照片對社團來說夠不夠好，因此她起初有些遲疑。

conversation.setting: A cafe manager interviews a student for a part-time barista position.

turns.text: Thanks for coming in today. Have you ever worked in a cafe before?
Not in a cafe, but I waited tables at a restaurant for a year, so I'm used to busy shifts.
Good. We mainly need someone to cover weekday mornings. Does that work for you?
Mornings are perfect, actually. My classes are all in the afternoon.
Excellent. Can you start training next Monday?
Definitely. I'll be here bright and early.

questions.q: Who is the woman most likely?
options: A university professor
A regular customer
A classmate of the man
A cafe manager
questions.why: 女子詢問應徵者的工作經驗並安排下週一的培訓，可推論她是咖啡店的經理。

questions.q: Why do the morning shifts suit the man?
options: He wants to earn more money
He lives close to the cafe
His classes are all in the afternoon
He dislikes working on weekends
questions.why: 男子說早班很完美，因為他的課都在下午，所以早班時間對他很方便。

conversation.setting: A co-worker asks another to swap shifts at their part-time job.

turns.text: Hey, are you working this Saturday? I need to ask you a favor.
I'm scheduled for the morning shift. What's up?
My cousin's graduation is Saturday morning. Could you take my shift, and I'll cover yours next week?
Let me think. I could do that, but only if you take my Friday night shift in return.
Deal. Friday night works for me. Thanks so much.
No problem. I'll let the supervisor know about the swap.

questions.q: Why does the man need a favor?
options: He wants to attend a graduation
He is feeling sick
He has a job interview
He wants a longer vacation
questions.why: 男子說星期六早上要參加表親的畢業典禮，所以他需要請人代班。

questions.q: What will the woman do next?
options: Cancel her shift completely
Tell the supervisor about the swap
Attend the graduation as well
Look for a new job
questions.why: 女子說她會通知主管換班的事，因此她接下來會去告知主管。

conversation.setting: Two roommates talk about late-night noise in their apartment.

turns.text: Can we talk for a second? I've been having trouble sleeping lately.
Sure, what's wrong?
The music from your room at night is a bit loud, and I have early classes.
Oh, I had no idea it carried through the walls. I'm really sorry.
It's okay. Maybe you could use headphones after ten?
Of course. I'll put on headphones from now on. Just tell me if it happens again.

questions.q: What is the woman's problem?
options: She lost her headphones
She is failing her classes
Loud music is keeping her awake
She wants to move out
questions.why: 女子說晚上房間傳來的音樂有點大聲，害她睡不好，所以問題是吵鬧的音樂讓她無法入睡。

questions.q: What does the man agree to do?
options: Move to another room
Stop listening to music completely
Buy the woman some earplugs
Use headphones at night
questions.why: 男子答應以後晚上戴耳機聽音樂，因此他同意在夜間使用耳機。

conversation.setting: A student invites a classmate to a party, but she politely declines.

turns.text: We're throwing a birthday party for Jenna on Friday night. You should come!
That sounds fun, and I'd really like to, but I have a big chemistry exam Saturday morning.
Oh, come on, just for an hour?
I wish I could, but I really need to study. This exam is a huge part of my grade.
Fair enough, I understand. I'll tell Jenna you say happy birthday.
Please do. Let's celebrate together some other time.

questions.q: What does the woman decide to do?
options: Go to the party for one hour
Politely turn down the invitation
Cancel her exam
Host her own party
questions.why: 女子說很想去，但因隔天早上有重要化學考試需要念書，委婉地拒絕了邀請。

questions.q: Why can the woman not attend?
options: She must study for an exam
She does not know Jenna
She is out of town
She dislikes parties
questions.why: 女子說星期六早上有重要的化學考試需要準備，所以無法參加派對。

conversation.setting: Two friends discuss a band's new album and plan to see them live.

turns.text: Have you listened to The Wanderers' new album yet? It's been on repeat for me all week.
Not the whole thing. Which song should I start with?
Definitely the one called Midnight Train. The guitar solo is unbelievable.
I'll check it out. You know, they're touring this summer, aren't they?
They are! They play our city in July. We should absolutely go.
Let's do it. I'll set a reminder for when tickets go on sale.

questions.q: What are the speakers mainly talking about?
options: Learning to play the guitar
A songwriting class
Finding a summer job
A band and its upcoming concert
questions.why: 兩人談論樂團的新專輯以及夏天的巡演，所以主要話題是這個樂團和他們即將舉行的演唱會。

questions.q: What will the man do?
options: Buy a new guitar
Write a review of the album
Set a reminder for the ticket sale
Skip the concert this year
questions.why: 男子說他會設定提醒，等售票時購買，所以他接下來會設定售票提醒。

conversation.setting: A student recruits a friend to volunteer at the campus spring festival.

turns.text: Are you free next weekend? The spring festival needs volunteers, and it's going to be huge this year.
What kind of help are they looking for?
Mostly setting up booths and handing out programs. It's only a few hours each day.
That doesn't sound too bad. Do volunteers get anything for it?
Yeah, a free T-shirt and access to all the concerts. Should I add your name to the list?
Sure, sign me up. It sounds like a good way to meet people.

questions.q: What is the man mainly doing?
options: Recruiting a volunteer for a festival
Selling tickets to a festival
Planning a concert tour
Designing festival T-shirts
questions.why: 男子告訴女子春季嘉年華需要志工並邀她加入，所以他主要是在招募活動志工。

questions.q: What will the man do next?
options: Cancel the festival
Add the woman's name to the list
Buy the woman a T-shirt
Set up all the booths alone
questions.why: 女子答應報名後，男子會把她的名字加到志工名單上。

conversation.setting: A librarian speaks with a student applying for a library assistant job.

turns.text: So, you're interested in the student assistant position at the library?
Yes, I love being around books, and I'm very organized.
The job involves reshelving books and helping students find materials. Are you comfortable with that?
Absolutely. I actually volunteered at my hometown library back in high school.
That's great experience. We'd need you about ten hours a week. Could you fill out this form?
Of course. I'll complete it right now.

questions.q: What position is the man applying for?
options: A bookstore cashier
A reading tutor
A library assistant
A festival volunteer
questions.why: 對話中提到圖書館學生助理的職缺，工作是整理書架與協助找資料，所以男子應徵的是圖書館助理。

questions.q: What will the man do next?
options: Reshelve some books
Visit his hometown library
Start work immediately
Fill out an application form
questions.why: 女子請他填寫表格，男子說馬上填，因此他下一步會填寫申請表。

conversation.setting: A restaurant worker tells the manager about a scheduling conflict.

turns.text: Excuse me, do you have a minute to look at next week's schedule with me?
Sure. Is there a problem?
You put me down for Wednesday evening, but I have a night class then.
Ah, I forgot about your class. Could you work Thursday evening instead?
Thursday evening works perfectly for me.
Great. I'll update the schedule and post it by tonight.

questions.q: Why does the man speak to the woman?
options: To quit his job
To fix a scheduling conflict
To ask for a raise
To drop his night class
questions.why: 男子指出自己被排在星期三晚上，但那時他有夜間課程，因此他找女子是為了解決排班衝突。

questions.q: What will the woman do?
options: Update the schedule
Cancel the man's shift
Take the night class herself
Hire a new worker
questions.why: 女子說她會更新班表並在今晚前公布，所以她接下來會更新排班表。

conversation.setting: Two roommates discuss sharing grocery costs and household chores.

turns.text: I think we should work out a better system for groceries and cleaning.
I agree. Lately I feel like I've been buying most of the food.
Same here with the dishes. How about we split the grocery costs down the middle?
That's fair. And we could take turns cleaning the kitchen each week.
Perfect. Let's make a shared list on our phones so nothing gets forgotten.
Good idea. I'll start the list tonight.

questions.q: What are the roommates trying to do?
options: Find a new apartment
Plan a dinner party
Hire a professional cleaner
Share costs and chores more fairly
questions.why: 兩人討論如何更公平地分攤買菜費用和打掃工作，所以他們想更公平地分擔開銷與家務。

questions.q: What will the man do tonight?
options: Buy all of the groceries
Clean the entire kitchen
Start a shared list on the phone
Move out of the apartment
questions.why: 男子說他今晚會開始建立共享清單，所以他接下來會建立清單。

conversation.setting: A student invites a friend on a hiking trip; she is unsure about the difficulty.

turns.text: A few of us are hiking Eagle Ridge on Sunday. Do you want to join us?
I'd like to, but I'm not sure I'm fit enough. I heard that trail is pretty steep.
There's actually an easier loop that's mostly flat. We can take that one.
Oh, that changes things. How long does the easy loop take?
About two hours, with plenty of breaks and some great views.
In that case, I'm happy to come. What should I bring?

questions.q: What is the man doing?
options: Inviting the woman on a hike
Describing a class project
Selling hiking gear
Complaining about a trail
questions.why: 男子邀請女子星期天一起去鷹嶺健行，所以他是在邀請女子參加健行。

questions.q: Why is the woman hesitant at first?
options: She has other plans on Sunday
She worries the trail is too difficult
She dislikes the man's friends
She has no hiking shoes
questions.why: 女子擔心自己體力不夠，因為聽說那條步道很陡，所以她起初猶豫是怕路線太難。

conversation.setting: Two friends plan to attend a student theater production this week.

turns.text: The drama club is putting on a play this Friday. I've heard it's really well done.
I didn't know they had a show coming up. Are tickets still available?
Yes, but the student tickets are selling fast. They're only five dollars.
That's a great deal. Should we get seats near the front?
Let's take the middle rows. The view is better for the whole stage.
Sounds good. I'll buy two tickets during lunch.

questions.q: What is the woman mainly telling the man?
options: She is acting in the play
The play has been canceled
About a play they could attend
That all the tickets are sold out
questions.why: 女子告訴男子戲劇社星期五有演出，票還買得到，所以她主要是在介紹一場他們可以去看的戲。

questions.q: What will the man do at lunch?
options: Join the drama club
Rehearse for the play
Sit in the front row
Buy two tickets
questions.why: 男子說他午餐時間會買兩張票，所以他接下來會在午餐時購票。

conversation.setting: A debate club member tries to recruit a student who fears public speaking.

turns.text: You made some really sharp points in class today. Have you thought about joining the debate club?
Me? I get so nervous speaking in front of people, though.
That's exactly why the club helps. We practice in small groups first.
Hmm, small groups do sound a lot less scary.
Exactly. And there's no pressure to compete until you feel ready.
Okay, I'll come to one practice and see how it feels.

questions.q: What is the man's purpose?
options: To ask the woman for debate tips
To recruit the woman for the debate club
To compete against the woman
To cancel a debate practice
questions.why: 男子稱讚女子上課的發言並邀她加入辯論社，所以他的目的是招募女子參加辯論社。

questions.q: Why is the woman reluctant?
options: She is nervous about public speaking
She has no free time
She dislikes the man
She already leads a club
questions.why: 女子說她在眾人面前講話會很緊張，所以她對加入有所遲疑。

conversation.setting: A parent interviews a student for a part-time math tutoring job.

turns.text: My son is really struggling with algebra. Have you tutored middle schoolers before?
Yes, I tutored two students last year, and both of them improved their grades.
That's reassuring. He gets frustrated easily, so patience is important.
I understand. I like to break problems into small steps so it feels manageable.
That sounds ideal. Could you come twice a week after school?
Twice a week works. I can start this Thursday.

questions.q: Who is the woman most likely?
options: A math teacher
A school principal
The man's classmate
A parent looking for a tutor
questions.why: 女子提到自己的兒子代數學得很吃力並詢問家教經驗，可推論她是想為孩子找家教的家長。

questions.q: What does the man say about his teaching style?
options: He gives a lot of homework
He teaches large groups
He breaks problems into small steps
He focuses on memorization
questions.why: 男子說他喜歡把題目拆解成小步驟，讓學生覺得比較容易上手，所以這是他的教學方式。

conversation.setting: A co-worker asks another to cover a shift during the holidays.

turns.text: I hate to ask, but is there any chance you could cover my shift on the 24th?
The 24th? That's the holiday. Why do you need it off?
My family is flying in that morning, and I want to pick them up from the airport.
I get it, family's important. I'm not doing anything that day, so sure.
Really? You're a lifesaver. I'll definitely return the favor.
Don't worry about it. Just enjoy the time with your family.

questions.q: What does the man ask the woman to do?
options: Cover his shift
Drive him to the airport
Join his family dinner
Switch jobs with him
questions.why: 男子請女子在24號幫他代班，所以他是請女子替他上班。

questions.q: How does the woman respond?
options: She refuses politely
She agrees to help
She asks for money
She suggests someone else
questions.why: 女子說那天她沒事，答應幫忙代班，所以她同意幫助男子。

conversation.setting: One roommate asks the other about having a guest stay for a few nights.

turns.text: Would it be alright if my cousin stayed with us for a couple of nights next week?
Hmm, where would she sleep? Our place is pretty small.
She'd just take the couch, and she's really tidy, I promise.
I guess that's fine, as long as it's only a couple of nights.
Definitely, just Tuesday and Wednesday. Thanks for being flexible.
No problem. Just give me a heads-up before she arrives.

questions.q: What does the woman want?
options: To buy a new couch
To move to a bigger place
To have her cousin stay over
To visit her cousin's home
questions.why: 女子詢問下週能否讓表妹來住幾晚，所以她想讓表妹過來借住。

questions.q: What does the man ask the woman to do?
options: Clean the apartment first
Pay some extra rent
Cancel the visit
Let him know before her cousin arrives
questions.why: 男子說沒問題，但請女子在表妹到達前先通知他一聲，所以他要求事先告知。

conversation.setting: A student invites a friend to a celebration dinner, but he has to decline.

turns.text: A group of us are going out for dinner Friday to celebrate the end of midterms. Want to join?
That sounds wonderful, but I actually picked up a shift at the store that evening.
Oh no, can't you get someone to cover it?
I wish I could, but I already asked for last week off, so I can't back out now.
That's too bad. We'll really miss you.
Save me a seat next time. I promise I'll be there.

questions.q: What does the man do?
options: Accept the dinner invitation
Decline because of work
Offer to cook dinner
Cancel his work shift
questions.why: 男子說那晚他要上班，無法赴約，所以他因為工作而婉拒了晚餐邀請。

questions.q: What can be inferred about the man's tone?
options: He is reluctant to say no
He is angry at the woman
He is uninterested in the dinner
He is relieved to be working
questions.why: 男子用「我也很想去」和「下次幫我留位子」等說法，顯示他其實很不情願拒絕，語氣帶著遺憾。

conversation.setting: Two friends plan to attend a weekend music festival and camp overnight.

turns.text: The summer music festival is back this year, and it's a whole weekend of shows.
I've always wanted to go to that! Is it far from here?
About two hours away. A lot of people camp there overnight to catch both days.
Camping sounds fun, but do we even have a tent?
My brother has one we can borrow. I'll ask him this weekend.
Perfect. Let's start planning what to pack.

questions.q: What are the speakers planning?
options: A camping-gear sale
A two-hour road race
A birthday party for the brother
A trip to a music festival
questions.why: 兩人討論要去參加為期一整個週末的音樂節並露營，所以他們在計劃去音樂節的旅程。

questions.q: How will they get a tent?
options: Buy one at the festival
Rent one online
Borrow the man's brother's tent
Sleep without one
questions.why: 男子說可以借他哥哥的帳篷，並會在週末去問他，所以他們會向他哥哥借帳篷。

conversation.setting: A student invites a friend to join an intramural volleyball team.

turns.text: Our intramural volleyball team is one player short. Would you want to fill the spot?
I haven't played volleyball since high school, though. I might be pretty rusty.
That's totally fine. It's just for fun, and we only practice once a week.
Once a week I can manage. When are the games?
Thursday evenings. The season starts in two weeks.
Alright, sign me up. It'll be nice to be active again.

questions.q: What is the woman's purpose?
options: To invite the man onto her team
To quit the volleyball team
To coach a high school team
To cancel the season
questions.why: 女子說她們的排球隊缺一名球員並邀男子加入，所以她的目的是邀請男子加入球隊。

questions.q: Why does the man hesitate at first?
options: He dislikes volleyball
He hasn't played in a long time
He is busy on Thursdays
He has an injury
questions.why: 男子說自從高中之後就沒打過排球，可能會生疏，所以他起初有些猶豫。

conversation.setting: A student asks a cafe owner about the hours and pay before taking a job.

turns.text: Before I decide, could you tell me a bit more about the hours and the pay?
Of course. The position is fifteen hours a week, mostly in the afternoons.
And what about the pay?
It starts at thirteen dollars an hour, plus a share of the tips.
That sounds fair. Do the hours ever change during exam season?
We're flexible then. Just give us a week's notice and we'll adjust the schedule.

questions.q: What does the man want to know?
options: The cafe's menu
The owner's name
The job's hours and pay
The location of the cafe
questions.why: 男子在決定前想多了解工時和薪水，所以他想知道這份工作的時數與待遇。

questions.q: What does the woman say about exam season?
options: The cafe closes during exams
Pay is reduced during exams
No schedule changes are allowed
Hours can be adjusted with notice
questions.why: 女子說考試季他們很有彈性，只要提前一週告知就能調整班表，所以工時可在事先通知後調整。

conversation.setting: Two retail co-workers arrange to trade their weekend shifts.

turns.text: Would you be willing to trade weekends with me? I'm scheduled this Saturday, but I have a wedding to go to.
Let me check. I'm off this Saturday, but I'm working next weekend.
Right, so if we swap, I'd take your shift next Saturday instead.
That works for me. A wedding sounds like a great reason.
Thank you so much. I'll write it on the schedule board so it's official.
Sounds good. See you on the floor.

questions.q: Why does the woman want to trade shifts?
options: She is sick this weekend
She has a wedding to attend
She wants extra work hours
She is going on vacation
questions.why: 女子說她這星期六被排班，但她要去參加婚禮，所以她想換班以便去參加婚禮。

questions.q: What will the woman do next?
options: Write the swap on the schedule board
Cancel her weekend plans
Ask the manager for time off
Work both weekends
questions.why: 女子說她會把換班寫在班表板上讓它正式生效，所以她接下來會在班表上登記。

conversation.setting: Two roommates discuss a high heating bill and the thermostat setting.

turns.text: Did you see this month's heating bill? It's much higher than usual.
Wow, that is steep. I wonder why it jumped so much.
I think the heat's been running all day, even when we're both out.
You're right. Maybe we should lower the thermostat when nobody's home.
Good idea. We could set a timer so it warms up right before we get back.
Let's try that this month and see if the bill goes down.

questions.q: What is the main topic of the conversation?
options: A broken heater
Moving to a warmer city
A roommate moving out
A high heating bill
questions.why: 兩人討論這個月的暖氣帳單比平常高很多，所以對話主題是偏高的暖氣帳單。

questions.q: What do the roommates decide to do?
options: Turn off the heat completely
Split the bill unevenly
Lower the thermostat when they are out
Call the landlord to complain
questions.why: 兩人決定沒人在家時把暖氣溫度調低，並設定計時器，所以他們決定外出時調低溫度。

conversation.setting: A customer is ordering lunch at a small restaurant and asks the server about the day's specials.

turns.text: Hi there, welcome in. Have you had a chance to look at the menu, or would you like to hear today's specials?
Actually, yeah, what are the specials today?
Today we've got a grilled salmon plate with roasted vegetables for fourteen dollars, and a mushroom pasta for eleven.
The salmon sounds good, but I'm trying not to spend too much. I'll go with the pasta.
Great choice. Would you like a soup or salad to start? It's an extra three dollars with any entrée.
I'll add the salad, thanks. And just water to drink.
Perfect. So that's the mushroom pasta with a side salad. I'll get that in for you right away.

questions.q: Why does the man choose the pasta instead of the salmon?
options: He does not eat fish.
He wants to spend less money.
The salmon is not available today.
He does not like vegetables.
questions.why: 男子說 'I'm trying not to spend too much',表示想少花點錢,而義大利麵(11 元)比鮭魚(14 元)便宜,故選 B。

questions.q: What will the woman most likely do next?
options: Bring the man a bowl of soup.
Change the man's drink to juice.
Cancel the pasta order.
Send the order to the kitchen.
questions.why: 女服務生最後說 'I'll get that in for you right away',表示要把餐點送進廚房下單,故選 D。男子選的是沙拉而非湯。

conversation.setting: A couple arrives at a busy restaurant without a reservation and speaks with the host about getting a table.

turns.text: Hi, do you have a table for two?
Sure, do you happen to have a reservation with us tonight?
No, we don't. Is that going to be a problem?
It's Friday night, so we're pretty full. The wait for a table for two is about forty minutes right now.
Oh, that's longer than we hoped. Is there anywhere we could sit sooner?
If you don't mind eating at the bar, there are two open seats there, and you can order off the full menu.
That works for us. We'll take the bar.
Great, follow me and I'll get you settled.

questions.q: How long is the wait for a regular table?
options: About forty minutes.
About fifteen minutes.
About an hour and a half.
There is no wait at all.
questions.why: 男子(帶位員)說 'the wait for a table for two is about forty minutes right now',故選 A。

questions.q: What does the couple decide to do?
options: Leave and find another restaurant.
Wait forty minutes for a table.
Sit at the bar to eat.
Make a reservation for another night.
questions.why: 女子說 'We'll take the bar',表示他們決定坐吧台用餐,故選 C。

conversation.setting: A diner tells the server about a food allergy while ordering a main dish.

turns.text: Before I order, I should mention I have a peanut allergy. Is that something the kitchen can work around?
Absolutely, we take allergies seriously. I'll flag it on your order and let the chef know. What were you thinking of getting?
The pad thai looked good, but doesn't that usually come with peanuts?
It does, but the chef can make it without them and use extra bean sprouts instead. Would that be okay?
That sounds perfect. Let's do that.
Great. I'll also make sure the kitchen prepares it on a clean surface, just to be safe.
I really appreciate that, thank you.

questions.q: Why does the man speak to the server before ordering?
options: To ask for a cheaper dish.
To request a different table.
To warn her about a food allergy.
To complain about slow service.
questions.why: 男子開頭說 'I have a peanut allergy',是要提醒服務生他對花生過敏,故選 C。

questions.q: How will the chef change the pad thai?
options: By leaving out the peanuts and adding bean sprouts.
By using a much spicier sauce.
By serving it with rice instead of noodles.
By adding a double portion of peanuts.
questions.why: 服務生說 'make it without them and use extra bean sprouts instead',即去掉花生、多加豆芽,故選 A。

conversation.setting: After dinner, a customer asks the server about splitting the bill between two cards.

turns.text: Here's your check whenever you're ready. No rush at all.
Thanks. Actually, is it possible to split this onto two cards?
Of course. Did you want it split evenly, or by what each person ordered?
Just evenly is fine. So that's about twenty-six each, right?
Exactly, the total's fifty-two, so twenty-six on each card. I'll run them separately.
Perfect. And is the tip already included?
No, the tip's not included, so feel free to add whatever you'd like when you sign.
Got it, thanks for letting me know.

questions.q: How much will be charged to each card?
options: Fifty-two dollars.
Thirteen dollars.
Sixty-two dollars.
Twenty-six dollars.
questions.why: 帳單總額 52 元,平分兩張卡,每張 26 元,故選 D。

questions.q: What does the woman say about the tip?
options: It has already been added to the bill.
It is not included in the total.
It must be paid in cash.
It is a fixed fifteen percent.
questions.why: 服務生說 'the tip's not included',表示小費未含在總額內,由客人自行決定,故選 B。

conversation.setting: A customer calls a pizzeria to place a takeout order over the phone.

turns.text: Thanks for calling Tony's Pizza, will this be for pickup or delivery?
Pickup, please. Can I get one large pepperoni pizza?
Sure thing. Would you like anything else with that? We've got a deal on garlic knots today.
Oh, why not, I'll add an order of garlic knots. How much is that all together?
That comes to eighteen dollars. Your order should be ready in about twenty-five minutes.
Twenty-five minutes, got it. I'll head over then.
Great, we'll see you soon. Can I get a name for the order?
It's under Rachel, thanks.

questions.q: Why is the woman calling the restaurant?
options: To place an order she will pick up.
To ask for a home delivery.
To complain about a past order.
To book a table for dinner.
questions.why: 女子說 'Pickup, please' 並點了披薩,是要下外帶自取的訂單,故選 A。

questions.q: When will the order be ready?
options: In about ten minutes.
In about forty minutes.
In about an hour.
In about twenty-five minutes.
questions.why: 男店員說 'ready in about twenty-five minutes',故選 D。

conversation.setting: A customer arrives to pick up a takeout order, but it is not ready yet.

turns.text: Hi, I'm here to pick up an order for Daniel.
Let me check. I'm sorry, Daniel, it looks like your order is running a little behind. We had a big rush come in.
Oh, okay. How much longer do you think it'll be?
Maybe another ten minutes. I'm really sorry for the wait.
That's alright. Is it okay if I just wait here?
Of course. And here, take a coupon for a free drink next time to make up for it.
Oh, that's very kind, thank you.

questions.q: What is the man trying to do?
options: Order a brand new meal.
Pick up an order he already placed.
Return some food he bought.
Ask for a table for dinner.
questions.why: 男子說 'I'm here to pick up an order for Daniel',表示要領取已下好的餐點,故選 B。

questions.q: What does the woman give the man because of the delay?
options: A full cash refund.
A free dessert.
A coupon for a free drink.
An extra side dish.
questions.why: 女店員說 'take a coupon for a free drink next time',故選 C。

conversation.setting: A customer opens a takeout bag at the counter and notices an item is missing.

turns.text: Excuse me, I just picked up this bag, but I think something's missing. I ordered two burritos and I only see one.
Let me take a look at your receipt. You're right, there should be two. I'm sorry about that.
No worries, could I get the second one?
Absolutely, I'll have the kitchen make it right now. It'll just be a couple of minutes.
Sure. Should I wait at the counter or over by the tables?
The counter's fine, I'll hand it to you as soon as it's up. Sorry again for the mix-up.
It's okay, these things happen.

questions.q: Why does the woman speak to the man?
options: She was charged the wrong price.
Her food has gone cold.
She wants to change her order.
One of her items is missing.
questions.why: 女子說 'I ordered two burritos and I only see one',表示少了一份餐點,故選 D。

questions.q: What will the man do next?
options: Have the kitchen make the missing burrito.
Give the woman a full refund.
Call the manager over to help.
Cancel the entire order.
questions.why: 男店員說 'I'll have the kitchen make it right now',表示要請廚房補做,故選 A。

conversation.setting: A shopper returns a blender to the customer service desk because it stopped working.

turns.text: Hi, I'd like to return this blender. I bought it last week and it already stopped working.
I'm sorry to hear that. Do you have your receipt with you?
Yeah, here it is.
Thank you. Since it's defective and you're within thirty days, I can give you a full refund or a replacement. Which would you prefer?
I think I'd just like a refund, actually.
No problem. I'll put the forty-five dollars back on your card. It may take a few days to show up.
That's fine. Thanks for making this easy.
Of course. You're all set.

questions.q: Why is the man at the service desk?
options: To buy a new blender.
To ask how to use a blender.
To return a blender that stopped working.
To complain about an employee.
questions.why: 男子說 'I'd like to return this blender... it already stopped working',是要退掉壞掉的果汁機,故選 C。

questions.q: How much money will be refunded to the man?
options: Thirty dollars.
Forty-five dollars.
Fifteen dollars.
Fifty-four dollars.
questions.why: 女店員說 'I'll put the forty-five dollars back on your card',故選 B。

conversation.setting: A customer wants to exchange a shirt for a larger size at a clothing store.

turns.text: Hi, I bought this shirt yesterday, but it's a little too tight. Could I exchange it for a bigger size?
Sure, no problem. What size did you get, and what size do you need?
I got a medium, but I think I need a large.
Let me check if we have it. Yes, we've got the large in the same color. Here you go.
Perfect. Is there any price difference?
Nope, same price, so it's an even exchange. I'll just need the tags and your receipt.
Here's the receipt, and the tags are still on.
Great, you're all set. Enjoy the shirt.

questions.q: Why does the woman want to exchange the shirt?
options: It is too small for her.
It is the wrong color.
It has a hole in it.
She does not like the style.
questions.why: 女子說 'it's a little too tight',並要從 medium 換成 large,表示尺寸太小,故選 A。

questions.q: What does the man say about the price?
options: The large costs five dollars more.
She will get a small refund.
There is no price difference.
She must pay the tax again.
questions.why: 男店員說 'same price, so it's an even exchange',表示沒有價差,故選 C。

conversation.setting: A customer returns an unopened jar to a supermarket because it was the wrong flavor.

turns.text: Hi, how can I help you today?
I bought this jar of pasta sauce this morning, but I grabbed the wrong one. I wanted the tomato basil, not the spicy one. Can I return it?
Sure, as long as it's unopened. Do you have the receipt?
I do, right here. It was four dollars.
Perfect. Would you like a refund, or do you want to swap it for the tomato basil one?
I'd rather just swap it, if the right one is in stock.
It is, aisle six on your left. Grab it and I'll do the exchange right here at this desk.
Great, I'll be right back.

questions.q: What mistake did the man make?
options: He lost his receipt.
He bought the wrong flavor of sauce.
He opened the jar by accident.
He was charged too much money.
questions.why: 男子說 'I grabbed the wrong one. I wanted the tomato basil, not the spicy one',表示買錯口味,故選 B。

questions.q: What will the man do next?
options: Get a cash refund and leave.
Speak to the store manager.
Wait for a new receipt.
Get the correct jar from aisle six.
questions.why: 男子選擇換貨,店員說正確的醬料在第六排,男子回 'I'll be right back',表示要去拿正確的那罐,故選 D。

conversation.setting: A customer tries to return a gift without a receipt and is offered store credit.

turns.text: Hi, I'd like to return this coffee mug. It was a gift, so I don't have the receipt.
I see. Without a receipt, I'm afraid I can't give you cash back, but I can offer store credit for the current price.
Okay, that works. How much would the credit be?
This mug is currently nine dollars, so that's what the credit would be.
That's fine. Can I use the credit today?
Absolutely. I'll put it on a gift card, and you can use it right now or anytime later.
Great, I'll actually use it today. Thanks.

questions.q: Why can't the woman get cash back?
options: The mug is already broken.
The store never does returns.
It has been too long since the purchase.
She does not have the receipt.
questions.why: 男店員說 'Without a receipt, I'm afraid I can't give you cash back',原因是沒有收據,故選 D。

questions.q: How much store credit will the woman receive?
options: Nine dollars.
Nineteen dollars.
Five dollars.
Ninety dollars.
questions.why: 男店員說 'This mug is currently nine dollars, so that's what the credit would be',故選 A。

conversation.setting: A shopper asks a supermarket employee where to find several items.

turns.text: Excuse me, could you tell me where I can find olive oil?
Sure, olive oil is in aisle four, with the cooking oils and vinegars.
Aisle four, great. And do you know if you carry the organic kind?
We do. It should be on the top shelf, near the specialty items.
Perfect. One more thing, where's the bread?
The bakery section is at the back of the store, just past the produce.
Thank you so much, you've been really helpful.
Happy to help. Let me know if you need anything else.

questions.q: Where can the man find olive oil?
options: In the bakery section.
In the produce area.
In aisle four.
At the front register.
questions.why: 女子說 'olive oil is in aisle four',故選 C。

questions.q: Who is the woman most likely?
options: Another customer who is shopping.
An employee of the supermarket.
The store manager's friend.
A delivery driver.
questions.why: 女子清楚各商品的擺放位置並主動指引,最可能是超市員工,故選 B。

conversation.setting: At the checkout, a customer notices an item rang up higher than the shelf price.

turns.text: Alright, your total comes to thirty-two fifty.
Wait, that seems high. The cereal was supposed to be on sale for three dollars, but I think it rang up as five.
Let me check. You're right, it scanned at the regular price. I'll fix that for you.
Thanks. The sign right by the shelf said three dollars.
I believe you. I'll adjust it, so that takes two dollars off. Your new total is thirty fifty.
Much better, thank you for catching that.
No problem at all. Sorry for the mix-up.

questions.q: Why does the man stop the cashier?
options: He forgot to grab an item.
The cereal was charged more than the sale price.
He wants to pay with a coupon.
He needs an extra bag.
questions.why: 男子說麥片特價 3 元卻收了 5 元,是價格與特價標示不符,故選 B。

questions.q: What is the new total after the fix?
options: Thirty dollars and fifty cents.
Thirty-two dollars and fifty cents.
Twenty-eight dollars.
Thirty-five dollars.
questions.why: 原價 32.50,減 2 元後店員說 'Your new total is thirty fifty',即 30.50,故選 A。

conversation.setting: A customer asks about a sale item that is out of stock and is offered a rain check.

turns.text: Hi, I came in for the chicken that's on sale this week, but the shelf is empty. Do you have any more in the back?
Let me check for you. I'm sorry, it looks like we're completely sold out for today.
Oh no, the sale ends tomorrow though.
I understand. I can write you a rain check, so you can still get the sale price when we restock, even after the sale ends.
That would be great. When do you expect more?
Our next delivery is Thursday, so it should be back by then.
Perfect. Let's do the rain check.
Sure, I'll write one up for you right now.

questions.q: Why is the man disappointed?
options: The chicken is far too expensive.
The store is closing early today.
He forgot his wallet at home.
The sale item is sold out.
questions.why: 男子想買特價雞肉但貨架空了,店員說 'completely sold out for today',故選 D。

questions.q: What does the woman offer the man?
options: A free food sample.
A different kind of meat.
A rain check for the sale price.
A full refund.
questions.why: 女員工說 'I can write you a rain check, so you can still get the sale price',故選 C。

conversation.setting: A cashier asks a customer whether they would like to sign up for a store rewards card.

turns.text: Do you have our rewards card today?
No, I don't. Is it worth signing up for?
Definitely. It's free, and you'd save ten percent on today's order just for joining.
Ten percent, really? What do I need to do?
Just your name, email, and a phone number. It takes about a minute.
Sure, let's do it. My total was fifty dollars, so I'd save five?
Exactly, five dollars off today, plus points on future purchases.
Sounds good, sign me up.

questions.q: Why does the man decide to sign up for the card?
options: He can save money on his order.
The card is required to pay.
He wants a free gift.
The cashier is an old friend.
questions.why: 店員說辦卡可省 10%,男子聽到能省 5 元後決定辦,是為了省錢,故選 A。

questions.q: How much will the man save today?
options: Ten dollars.
Five dollars.
Fifteen dollars.
Fifty dollars.
questions.why: 總額 50 元,省 10% 即 5 元,店員也確認 'five dollars off today',故選 B。

conversation.setting: A customer arrives at a store's pickup counter to collect an online grocery order.

turns.text: Hi, I'm here to pick up an online order. The name is Carter.
Welcome. Can I see your order confirmation? Just the number is fine.
Sure, it's order two-two-nine.
Got it, order two-two-nine for Carter. Let me grab it from the cooler. Did you order any frozen items?
Yeah, a bag of frozen vegetables and some ice cream.
Okay, those are packed separately to keep them cold. Here's everything, three bags total.
Three bags, perfect. Thanks so much.
You're welcome. Have a great day.

questions.q: Why is the man at the counter?
options: To return some frozen food.
To apply for a job.
To collect an order he bought online.
To ask for a store map.
questions.why: 男子說 'I'm here to pick up an online order',是要領取網購訂單,故選 C。

questions.q: How many bags is the man's order?
options: One bag.
Two bags.
Five bags.
Three bags.
questions.why: 女店員說 'Here's everything, three bags total',故選 D。

conversation.setting: A customer picking up an online order learns that one item was replaced with a substitute.

turns.text: Hi, I'm picking up an order for Nguyen.
Sure. Before you go, I want to let you know we made one substitution. You ordered whole milk, but we were out, so we gave you two percent instead.
Oh, okay. Is it the same size and price?
Same size, and the two percent was actually a little cheaper, so we refunded you the difference, about fifty cents.
That's fine, two percent works for me. Everything else is there?
Yes, everything else is exactly as ordered. You're all set.
Great, thanks for letting me know.

questions.q: What is the man telling the woman about?
options: Her whole order was canceled.
One item was replaced with a substitute.
The store has run out of bags.
Her payment did not go through.
questions.why: 男店員說 'we made one substitution',把全脂牛奶換成低脂,故選 B。

questions.q: What happened with the price of the milk?
options: She was refunded about fifty cents.
She was charged fifty cents more.
The price stayed exactly the same.
She got the milk for free.
questions.why: 男店員說低脂較便宜 'we refunded you the difference, about fifty cents',故選 A。

conversation.setting: A customer calls the store from the parking lot about a curbside pickup order.

turns.text: Thanks for calling, this is the curbside line. Are you here for a pickup?
Yes, I'm in the parking lot for a curbside order, but I'm not sure where to go.
No problem. Do you see the numbered signs along the front of the store?
Oh yes, I see them now.
Just pull into any open spot with a number and tell me which one you're in. I'll bring your order out.
I'm in spot number seven, in a blue car.
Great, spot seven, blue car. I'll be right out with your order.
Thank you, I'll wait right here.

questions.q: Why is the woman calling the store?
options: To place a brand new order.
To cancel her pickup order.
To ask about the store hours.
To get help finding the pickup area.
questions.why: 女子說 'I'm not sure where to go',是要問取貨區怎麼走,故選 D。

questions.q: What will the man do next?
options: Move the woman's car for her.
Cancel the order.
Bring the order out to spot seven.
Transfer the call to a manager.
questions.why: 男店員說 'I'll be right out with your order' 到 7 號車位,故選 C。

conversation.setting: A customer orders a customized latte at a coffee shop counter.

turns.text: Hi, what can I get started for you?
Can I get a medium latte, but with oat milk instead of regular?
Sure, oat milk latte. Anything else, like a flavor shot?
Yeah, add one pump of vanilla. And can you make it extra hot?
One pump of vanilla, extra hot, you got it. That'll be five dollars. Can I get a name for the cup?
It's Jordan.
Thanks, Jordan. It'll be up at the end of the counter in just a minute.
Perfect, thank you.

questions.q: How does the man want his latte made?
options: With oat milk and a shot of vanilla.
With regular milk and no flavor.
Iced with two sugars.
Decaf with whipped cream.
questions.why: 男子要 'oat milk instead of regular' 與 'one pump of vanilla',故選 A。

questions.q: What will the man most likely do next?
options: Pay at a different register.
Leave the coffee shop immediately.
Order a sandwich as well.
Wait at the end of the counter for his drink.
questions.why: 店員說 'It'll be up at the end of the counter',男子會去櫃台尾端等取飲料,故選 D。

conversation.setting: A customer at a coffee shop receives the wrong drink and speaks to the barista.

turns.text: Excuse me, I think this might be the wrong drink. I ordered a hot chocolate, but this tastes like coffee.
Oh, I'm so sorry about that. Let me check the order. You're right, this was meant for someone else. I'll make your hot chocolate right away.
Thanks. Do you need this cup back?
No, you can keep it or toss it, don't worry. Your hot chocolate will just take a minute.
No problem. Should I wait right here?
Yes, right here is fine. And here's a punch on your loyalty card for the trouble.
Oh, thanks, that's nice of you.

questions.q: Why does the man talk to the barista?
options: He wants to order a second drink.
His drink is much too cold.
He was given the wrong drink.
He lost his loyalty card.
questions.why: 男子說 'I ordered a hot chocolate, but this tastes like coffee',是拿錯飲料,故選 C。

questions.q: What will the woman do next?
options: Give the man a full refund.
Make the man a hot chocolate.
Call the next customer in line.
Take the wrong cup back.
questions.why: 女店員說 'I'll make your hot chocolate right away',故選 B。

conversation.setting: A customer tries to order a pastry that is sold out and picks an alternative.

turns.text: Hi, could I get a blueberry muffin and a small coffee?
I'm sorry, we just sold the last blueberry muffin. We still have banana bread and chocolate croissants, though.
Oh, that's too bad. I'll take a chocolate croissant instead, then.
Good choice. So that's a chocolate croissant and a small coffee. That comes to six fifty.
Here's my card. Do you warm up the croissant?
We can, yeah. I'll heat it up for you. It'll be ready in just a moment.
Great, thank you.

questions.q: Why does the woman change her order?
options: The muffin is too expensive.
The blueberry muffins are sold out.
She does not like blueberries.
The coffee is unavailable.
questions.why: 店員說 'we just sold the last blueberry muffin',藍莓瑪芬賣完了,故選 B。

questions.q: How much does the woman's order cost?
options: Six dollars and fifty cents.
Five dollars and fifteen cents.
Six dollars and fifteen cents.
Seven dollars and fifty cents.
questions.why: 店員說 'That comes to six fifty',即 6.50 元,故選 A。

conversation.setting: A customer places an order for a custom birthday cake at a bakery.

turns.text: Hi, I'd like to order a birthday cake for this Saturday. Is that possible?
Absolutely. What size and flavor were you thinking?
A medium chocolate cake, enough for about ten people. Can you write 'Happy Birthday, Mia' on it?
Of course. A medium chocolate cake serves about twelve, so that'll be plenty. The custom message is included.
Great. How much is it, and when should I pick it up?
It's thirty-five dollars, and you can pick it up anytime after ten on Saturday morning.
Perfect. I'll come by around eleven.
Sounds good. I'll have it ready for you.

questions.q: Why is the man at the bakery?
options: To return a cake he bought.
To apply for a baking job.
To buy bread for dinner.
To order a custom birthday cake.
questions.why: 男子說 'I'd like to order a birthday cake',是要訂做生日蛋糕,故選 D。

questions.q: How much will the cake cost?
options: Fifteen dollars.
Twenty-five dollars.
Thirty-five dollars.
Fifty dollars.
questions.why: 女店員說 'It's thirty-five dollars',故選 C。

announcement.setting: A professor makes an announcement at the start of a biology lecture.
announcement.text: Before we begin, a quick note about next week. Thursday's lecture will be moved from this room to Hall B, which is just across the hallway. Also, because of the campus holiday on Monday, our lab session will shift to Wednesday at three o'clock instead. Please update your calendars, and email me if that new lab time creates any conflicts.

questions.q: Where will Thursday's lecture be held?
options: In the same room as usual.
In Hall B across the hallway.
In the main science library.
In the lab downstairs.
questions.why: 教授說週四改到走廊對面的 B 廳。

questions.q: Why is the lab session being moved?
options: Because of a campus holiday on Monday.
Because the lab is being renovated.
Because too many students enrolled.
Because the professor will be traveling.
questions.why: 因為週一是校園假日,實驗課才改到週三。

announcement.setting: A teaching assistant explains the rules before a final exam.
announcement.text: Please listen carefully before we hand out the exam. You'll have exactly ninety minutes, and no one may leave during the first thirty. Put your bags and phones at the front of the room. You may use a calculator, but not the one on your phone. When you finish, stay seated and raise your hand, and a proctor will come collect your paper.

questions.q: How long do students have to complete the exam?
options: Thirty minutes.
Sixty minutes.
Ninety minutes.
A full two hours.

announcement.setting: A recorded message plays over the speakers in the university library.

As a reminder, the third floor is a designated silent study zone, so please keep all conversations and phone calls in the lobby or the group rooms on the second floor. The library closes at eleven tonight, but during finals week it will stay open until two in the morning. Thank you for helping us keep the space quiet.

options: On the third floor, if they whisper.
Outside the building entirely.
Anywhere, as long as it is brief.
In the lobby or second-floor group rooms.
questions.why: 電話要在大廳或二樓的討論室講。

At exactly midnight.
At one in the morning.
At two in the morning.
questions.why: 期末週圖書館會開到凌晨兩點。

announcement.setting: A department head speaks at a student awards ceremony.
announcement.text: It's my great pleasure to recognize this year's outstanding students. First, congratulations to Maria Chen, whose research on coral reefs won first place at the regional science fair last month. I'd also like to thank the twelve volunteers who ran our community tutoring program every Saturday. Because of their work, over two hundred local children received free math help this year. Please join me in applauding them.

questions.q: What did Maria Chen do?
options: She organized the tutoring program.
She volunteered every single Saturday.
She donated money to the school.
She won first place for coral reef research.
questions.why: Maria Chen 的珊瑚礁研究拿了地區科展第一名。

options: Two hundred of them.
Around fifty of them.
Twelve of them.
Just two of them.
questions.why: 有十二位志工每週六參與義務輔導。

announcement.setting: An announcement is broadcast across the campus recreation center.
announcement.text: Good afternoon, everyone. Please note that the swimming pool will be closed this Friday for its annual cleaning, reopening Saturday at six in the morning. In the meantime, the fitness center and the indoor track remain open as usual. Members who reserved a Friday swim lesson can rebook online, and the front desk will refund your session fee if you prefer.

options: Because of a swim competition.
Due to a broken water heater.
For a private members' event.
For its annual cleaning.

announcement.setting: A professor gives instructions about an upcoming museum field trip.
announcement.text: A few reminders about our museum trip on Friday. The bus leaves from the main gate at eight sharp, so please arrive by seven forty-five. Bring your student ID for the group discount, and wear comfortable shoes, since we'll be walking for most of the morning. Lunch is not provided, but there's a café inside where you can buy sandwiches and coffee.

questions.q: What time should students arrive at the main gate?
options: At seven forty-five.
At eight fifteen.
At seven o'clock sharp.
At eight thirty.

To buy lunch at the café.
To receive the group discount.
To enter the classroom later.
questions.why: 帶學生證是為了拿團體折扣。

announcement.setting: A resident advisor posts a spoken reminder in a dormitory.

Also, the laundry room now takes only the campus card, not coins, so load your card at the machine near the entrance. Maintenance will inspect the smoke detectors next Tuesday between nine and noon.

At eleven at night.
At midnight.
At nine in the evening.
questions.why: 週末的安靜時段從午夜開始。

The campus card.
A mobile app.
A special token.
questions.why: 洗衣間現在只收校園卡,不收硬幣。

announcement.setting: The president of a student club makes an announcement at a meeting.

Next, our meeting time is changing: starting in April, we'll gather on Tuesdays at five instead of Mondays. Finally, we still need three more people to help run the spring charity event, so see me afterward if you're interested.

options: They won first place overall.
They placed second.
They failed to qualify.
They came in dead last.

options: Meetings will move to Tuesdays at five.
Meetings will be canceled during April.
Meetings will move to Mondays at six.
Meetings will now be held twice a week.

Next Friday morning
This Tuesday at noon
Next Monday by 5 p.m.

Through the online portal
By email to the professor
At the department office

Phones must be switched off and placed in your bag during the test.

In Hall 204
In the library
In the main auditorium

Two students will lead the discussion, and I've posted the sign-up sheet outside my office. Please add your name by Wednesday, because I can only take six volunteers this time.

Chapters three and four

By Friday
By the next seminar

At the beginning of class
During the lunch break
After class in the office

questions.q: What does the professor advise about eating during the task?

Keep eating normally for honest data
Skip snacks to simplify records
Only record main meals
questions.why: 老師建議照常飲食,因為誠實的數據更有用。

announcement.setting: in a political science class
announcement.text: Let's finalise next week's debate. The motion is that voting should be compulsory, and I've split the class into two teams on the noticeboard. Prepare your arguments as a team, but be ready to think on your feet.

Room 12 on Wednesday at 11

questions.q: How long does each speaker have?
options: Two minutes
Three minutes
Four minutes
Five minutes
questions.why: 每位發言者有四分鐘。

announcement.setting: in a film studies course
announcement.text: A quick note about this week's screening. We're showing the film on Thursday evening at 7 in lecture theatre A, and it runs for two hours. Attendance is expected, since Friday's discussion depends on it. If you truly can't make it, the library has a copy on the second floor you can watch instead. Either way, bring notes on the opening scene to class.

Wednesday evening
Thursday at 5
Thursday evening at 7

On the course website
In the professor's office
At the campus cinema
questions.why: 若無法出席,可到圖書館二樓看館藏。

announcement.setting: in a first-year study skills session
announcement.text: Before the break, two reminders. First, the extra revision session is this Friday at 3 in room 7, and it's open to anyone who wants help with the essay. Second, the library changes its hours next week, closing at 9 instead of 11 each evening. Plan your study time around that. Bring your questions on Friday, and I'll try to answer as many as I can.

questions.q: When and where is the extra revision session?

Friday at 5 in room 11
Monday at 3 in room 9
Friday at 3 in room 7

It will open earlier
It will close on weekends
It will move to a new building
questions.why: 圖書館下週改為晚上九點閉館,而非十一點。

announcement.setting: A recorded announcement plays over the main library speakers.
announcement.text: Attention, students. As final exams approach, the main library will extend its hours starting Monday. We'll now stay open until 2 a.m. on weekdays, and the third-floor quiet zone will remain accessible around the clock. Please note that the coffee cart near the entrance closes at midnight. Bring your student ID, since the side doors require a card swipe after 10 p.m. Good luck with your studies.

Starting Monday
After midnight
Next month

options: The quiet zone
The side doors
The library
The coffee cart
questions.why: 入口附近的咖啡餐車在午夜關閉。

announcement.setting: A dining services message is broadcast in the student union.
announcement.text: Good morning, everyone. The north dining hall will be closed this Thursday and Friday for a deep kitchen cleaning. During those two days, all meal-plan swipes will be accepted at the south cafeteria instead, which opens early at 7 a.m. The salad bar and the grill station will operate as usual, but the pizza oven stays offline until Saturday. We apologize for the inconvenience and thank you for your patience.

questions.q: Which two days will the north dining hall be closed?
options: Monday and Tuesday
Wednesday only
Saturday and Sunday

The pizza oven
The kitchen
The student union
questions.why: 那兩天所有餐卡刷卡改到南餐廳。

announcement.setting: An announcement echoes through the recreation center.
announcement.text: Hey, members. Just a quick heads-up from the fitness staff. The upstairs cardio room will be closed tomorrow from 8 a.m. until noon while technicians service the treadmills and rowing machines. The weight room downstairs stays fully open during that time. Also, the two spin bikes near the window are out of order and won't be repaired until next Wednesday. If you need a locker, remember the day lockers are cleared out every evening at 10.

questions.q: What time does the cardio room reopen tomorrow?
options: At 8 a.m.
At noon
At 10 p.m.
Next Wednesday

options: Tomorrow morning
This evening
Next Wednesday
At noon

announcement.setting: A transit announcement plays at the campus bus stop.
announcement.text: Attention, riders. Beginning next Monday, the Blue Line shuttle will run on a new schedule. Buses will depart from the main gate every fifteen minutes during the morning rush, instead of every twenty. The last evening shuttle now leaves at 11:30 p.m. rather than 11. Please be aware that the stop outside the science building is moving one block north, closer to the parking garage. Updated route maps are posted at every shelter.

questions.q: How often will morning buses depart under the new schedule?
options: Every fifteen minutes
Every twenty minutes
Every thirty minutes
Every hour

One block north
Inside the garage
To the main gate

announcement.setting: A parking services notice is announced across the lot.
announcement.text: Good afternoon, drivers. Lot C behind the engineering building will be closed all day this Saturday for repaving. Vehicles left overnight Friday must be removed before 6 a.m., or they will be towed at the owner's expense. Permit holders may park in Lot F, near the stadium, at no extra charge that day. The work should finish by Sunday evening, and the lot will reopen Monday morning. Thank you for cooperating.

questions.q: By what time must overnight cars leave Lot C?
options: By midnight
By 6 a.m. Saturday
By Sunday evening
By Monday

options: Lot C
The engineering building
The main gate
Lot F near the stadium
questions.why: 持證者當天可免費停在體育場旁的 F 停車場。

announcement.setting: A facilities alert is broadcast in the residence hall.
announcement.text: Residents, please listen carefully. The water supply in Hawthorne Hall will be shut off Wednesday from 9 a.m. to 3 p.m. so crews can replace an aging pipe on the fourth floor. During the shutdown, restrooms on floors one through five will be unavailable. A temporary water station and portable toilets will be set up in the lobby. We strongly recommend filling a bottle the night before. Normal service resumes by dinnertime.

Showers

announcement.setting: A message plays in the student health center waiting area.
announcement.text: Hello, everyone. Starting the first of next month, the student health center is changing its hours. We will open at 8 a.m. on weekdays, one hour earlier than before, and close at 6 p.m. On Saturdays we'll now be available from 10 to 2 for urgent visits only. The pharmacy window, however, keeps its current schedule. Appointments can still be booked online or by calling the front desk. Please arrive ten minutes early.

Two hours earlier
It stays the same
One hour earlier

Pharmacy pickups
Online appointments
questions.why: 週六十點到兩點只看緊急就診。

announcement.setting: An announcement plays near the library circulation desk.
announcement.text: Attention, patrons. The book return chute on the east side of the library will be sealed for repairs beginning Tuesday. Until it reopens, please drop all returns at the main circulation desk on the ground floor. Items due during this period will not accrue late fees, so there's no need to worry. The self-checkout machines remain fully functional. If you have questions about a hold, a librarian is available at the help counter until 9 p.m.

They are automatically lost

announcement.setting: A dining announcement plays in the food court.
announcement.text: Hi, students. We're excited to welcome a new noodle bar to the east food court, opening this Friday at 11 a.m. To celebrate, the first two hundred customers will receive a free drink with any order. The smoothie stand next door is relocating to the second floor and will reopen Monday. Please note the food court now closes at 9 p.m. on weeknights, thirty minutes later than last semester. Come hungry and enjoy.

The east court entrance
The noodle bar
Outside

announcement.setting: An announcement plays in the campus gym lobby.

Sunrise yoga will meet Tuesdays and Thursdays at 6:30 a.m. in Studio B, and evening kickboxing runs Mondays at 7 p.m. in the main hall. Both classes are free for members, but space is limited to twenty people, so sign up at the front desk in advance. The rock-climbing wall will be closed for inspection this Friday only.

Fifteen
Twenty
Fifty

announcement.setting: A transit alert plays inside the shuttle bus.
announcement.text: Good morning, passengers. Due to construction on Elm Street, the Green Line will detour starting Thursday. The library stop and the dormitory stop will be skipped for about two weeks. Instead, board at the temporary stop beside the student center, which runs every ten minutes. Riders heading to the medical campus should transfer to the Red Line at the main gate. We expect normal service to return by the end of the month. Thanks for your patience.

announcement.setting: A parking office announcement plays over campus radio.
announcement.text: Attention, commuters. Parking permits for the fall semester go on sale next Monday through the online portal. Prices remain unchanged from last year, but a new discounted evening permit is available for students who only attend after 5 p.m. Remember that all vehicles must display the new sticker by September 1st, or you risk a forty-dollar fine. The parking office window will be open extra hours next week, from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. daily, to answer questions.

Faculty members
Weekend visitors
Students attending only after 5 p.m.

announcement.setting: A facilities notice plays in an academic building lobby.

During those two days, please use the elevator in the west wing or the stairs. We apologize to anyone with mobility needs; staff at the front desk can arrange assistance if you call ahead. The freight elevator remains available for deliveries only. Full service should resume Wednesday morning at the latest.

announcement.setting: An announcement plays inside the campus bookstore.
announcement.text: Hello, shoppers. The campus bookstore is holding its end-of-semester sale this week, with all clothing and gifts twenty percent off. On Friday only, the store will stay open late until 10 p.m. for the sale. Textbook buyback begins Monday at the lower-level counter, so bring your used books and a photo ID. Please note the bookstore will be completely closed on the upcoming holiday weekend. Regular hours resume the following Tuesday.

A parking permit

During those hours, recreational swimmers may use the smaller training pool instead. Lap lanes in the main pool reopen to everyone after 5. Also, the hot tub is temporarily closed for chemical balancing and should reopen this Saturday. Remember, a lifeguard must be on duty before anyone enters the water. Swim safely.

announcement.setting: An announcement plays in the technology commons.

The computer lab on the third floor will undergo a software upgrade this Wednesday night, so it will close early at 6 p.m. All machines should be up and running again by Thursday at 8 a.m. Please save and back up any files stored locally, because the upgrade will erase the hard drives. The printers in the hallway stay available around the clock. If you need help, a technician is on call at extension 4500.

announcement.setting: An announcement plays in the library study wing.
announcement.text: Attention, students. The group study rooms on the second floor now require an online reservation. Walk-in use is no longer allowed during peak hours, from noon to 8 p.m. Each room can be booked for a maximum of two hours per day. If you're more than fifteen minutes late, the system releases your room to the next group. Rooms 210 and 211 have new video screens for presentations. Reserve through the library website using your student login.

Four hours

The room is released to the next group

announcement.setting: An announcement plays in the newly renovated student lounge.

For the first week, all coffee drinks are just one dollar before 10 a.m. The cafe seats sixty people and offers a quiet corner with power outlets at every table. Hours are 7 a.m. to 9 p.m. on weekdays and 9 to 5 on weekends. Take the express elevator to the sixth floor, and enjoy the view.

Fifty
Sixty

After 9 p.m.

announcement.setting: An announcement plays at the recreation center desk.
announcement.text: Good afternoon, members. Please note the recreation center will operate on a holiday schedule this coming Friday. We'll open late at 10 a.m. and close early at 4 p.m. that day. The indoor track and basketball courts stay open the whole time, but the sauna and towel service will be unavailable. Personal training sessions scheduled for Friday afternoon are being moved to Saturday. All normal hours return the following Monday. Thank you for understanding.

announcement.setting: An announcement plays in the residence hall laundry room.

Starting today, all machines are card-operated, and the old coin slots have been removed. You can add money to your laundry card at the kiosk near the mailroom. A single wash now costs one dollar and fifty cents. Please remove your clothes promptly, since the room gets busy on Sunday evenings. Report any broken machine to the front office.

announcement.setting: An announcement plays in the campus mail center.

Bring your tracking number and student ID to collect a parcel. Packages left unclaimed for more than fourteen days will be returned to the sender. The window is closed on weekends, but the self-service lockers outside are accessible anytime with a code. Thanks.

6 p.m.
7 p.m.
9 p.m.

announcement.setting: A campus IT announcement plays in the library.

We scheduled this overnight to affect as few people as possible. Wired connections in the labs should stay online throughout. If problems continue after 6 a.m., please contact the help desk at extension 2200. We appreciate your patience while we improve the network.

Saturday night
Monday morning
All weekend

Mobile data

announcement.setting: An announcement plays in the career services office.
announcement.text: Hi, students. The career center is hosting a resume workshop this Thursday at 4 p.m. in Room 120. Bring a printed draft of your resume and a laptop if you have one. Space is limited to thirty students, so register on our website by Wednesday noon. Employers from three local companies will attend the job fair next Tuesday in the gymnasium, so dress professionally. Drop-in advising hours have moved to mornings, now 9 to 12 daily.

questions.q: By when must students register for the workshop?
options: Thursday at 4 p.m.
Next Tuesday
Friday morning
Wednesday noon

Room 120
The career center
Online

announcement.setting: An announcement plays in the academic support center.
announcement.text: Good evening, students. The math tutoring center is extending its hours during midterm week. Beginning this Sunday, tutors will be available from 10 a.m. to 10 p.m. every day, including the weekend. Walk-ins are welcome, but appointments get priority, so book a slot online. The writing center next door keeps its regular schedule, closing at 6. Free review sessions for calculus will run Monday and Wednesday nights in Room 305. Bring your textbook and questions.

questions.q: During midterm week, when does the math tutoring center close?
options: At 10 p.m.
At 6 p.m.
At 8 p.m.
At midnight

announcement.setting: An announcement plays in the campus art museum.

Sunday
Monday
Tuesday

questions.q: When does the guided tour with the artists happen?

On opening night at 7
Every Monday
Sunday afternoon
